You are reviewing a merge that just landed on master in the nostr-multi-platform repo (a Rust multiplatform framework for Nostr apps, currently building toward v1 per docs/plan.md). The doctrine rubric is D0-D8 in docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md §1.5 and docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:
- D0 kernel never grows app nouns
- D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders
- D2 negentropy first
- D3 outbox automatic
- D4 single writer per fact
- D5 snapshots bounded by what's open
- D6 errors never cross FFI (become toast state fields)
- D7 capabilities report, never decide
- D8 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, =60 Hz/view, working-set bounded, zero per-event alloc after warmup)

File-size limit: 300 LOC soft, 500 LOC hard (AGENTS.md).

+# NMP Builder Guide — PLAN
+
+> **Plan only.** TOC + per-section briefs. Parallel writer agents will produce `NN-*.md`. No prose docs here. Two audiences: **builders** (humans coming from NDK / Applesauce / raw `nostr-sdk`) and **agents** (LLMs extending NMP). Authoring rules: each file ≤ 300 LOC (AGENTS.md); cross-links `[NN — title](NN-name.md)`; cite `path:line` only if file exists today on master; distill not duplicate; ≥3 anti-patterns and ≥2 concrete deliverables per section.
+
+## Status legend (citation discipline)
+
+| Status | Writer may cite | Must NOT cite |
+|---|---|---|
+| **SHIPS** | `crates/**/*.rs:line`, `apps/**/*.rs:line`, `ios/**/*.swift:line`, any `docs/**` | speculative paths |
+| **LANDED** | `docs/design/**/*.md`§, `docs/decisions/000N-*.md`, `docs/plan/m*.md`, plus partial `crates/` cites for scaffolded-but-incomplete code | `path:line` for code paths that don't exist on master |
+| **PLANNED** | `docs/plan/m*.md`, ADRs, `docs/design/**/*.md`, scope memo | any `crates/`/`apps/`/`ios/` path absent from master |
+
+Doctrine canon: `docs/product-spec/doctrine.md` (canonical D0–D8 file) + `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 (in-page restatement). Research synthesis: `docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md` + `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` + `docs/research/{ndk,applesauce,highlighter}/`. Reality on master HEAD (`a92ed2e`): kernel substrate + planner + LMDB store + subscription pool + NIP-42 auth gate + publish engine **all SHIP**; protocol crates `nmp-signers` / `nmp-nip29` / `nmp-nip42` / `nmp-nip77` **all SHIP**; `nmp-highlighter-core` is a stub (M11.5 step 0); `apps/podcast/{podcast-core,podcast-feeds,podcast-audio,podcast-llm,podcast-rag}` shipped as scaffolds; `ios/NmpStress` (1,375 LOC) is the live shell; `ios/NmpPodcast` + `ios/NmpHighlighter` are tiny skeletons. FFI today is raw C JSON-over-string (`crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`); UniFFI migration is M14 (PLANNED). M0–M8 + M10.5 + M11 + M11.5 are tracked DONE in `docs/perf/orchestration-log.md` HB31.
+

+
+## Per-section briefs
+
+Compact format. Each section: **covers** (1 line) · **cite** (file:line refs) · **deliver** (2–3 concrete artifacts) · **anti** (3 anti-patterns) · **xref** (other sections). `prereqs` implied by xref. `outcomes` implied by covers.
+
+### 00 — How to read this guide  *(SHIPS · both · 500)*
+- covers: who the guide is for; SHIPS/LANDED/PLANNED legend; reading paths for builder vs agent; doc-bug filing route via section 27.
+- cite: this `PLAN.md`; `README.md`; `AGENTS.md`; `docs/plan/status.md`.
+- deliver: 2 ordered reading paths ("ship an app" / "extend the kernel"); ASCII graph of section dependencies.
+- anti: reading sections out of order; copying PLANNED code into a real app; assuming a section is wrong when status flag explicitly marks aspirational content.
+- xref: 01, 02, 22.
+
+### 01 — What NMP is + why it exists  *(SHIPS · builders · 1800)*
+- covers: one-paragraph definition; the "make broken Nostr apps impossible" thesis; concrete contrast with NDK / Applesauce / raw `nostr-sdk` (one paragraph each); the four arcs (M0–M10 social · M10.5 hardening · M11 kernel-boundary proof via podcast · M11.5 Highlighter + NIP-29 · M13–M17 release); status snapshot (M0–M8 + M10.5 + M11 + M11.5 DONE per HB31).
+- cite: `README.md:1-117`; `docs/aim.md:9-89`; `docs/plan.md:30-52`; `docs/plan/status.md:1-60`; `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md:1-65`; `docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md`; `docs/research/ndk/missing-features-for-nmp.md`; `docs/research/applesauce/missing-features-for-nmp.md`.
+- deliver: comparison table NDK/Applesauce/raw `nostr-sdk`/NMP × 6 axes (state ownership, outbox, kind:3 auto, reactivity, signers, FFI); "what NMP is NOT" box; "deferred to post-v1" callout (M9 DMs, M12 Wallet).
+- anti: framing NMP as "Rust NDK"; implying NDK feature parity (NDK has DMs/Wallet, NMP defers both); implying v1 ships everything in `docs/aim.md` (post-v1 scope memo deferrals stand).
+- xref: 02, 03, 25.
+

+
+## Writer-agent process
+
+1. Read this PLAN + your section's brief + cited prereqs (briefs or drafted sections) before drafting.
+2. Honor the `status` field strictly. SHIPS may cite `crates/`/`apps/`/`ios/` `path:line`; LANDED cites design + ADRs + partial code; PLANNED cites plan files + ADRs only.
+3. Verify every `path:line` you cite by Reading the file in the same commit (`a92ed2e` or later on master).
+4. Keep your file ≤ 300 LOC. If exceeded, split per the brief's hint (e.g. 05a/05b) and update the TOC in a follow-up.
+5. Include ≥3 anti-patterns and ≥2 concrete deliverables from the brief.
+6. End every section with `See also:` listing `xref` exactly as `[NN — title](NN-name.md)`.
+7. Distill — do not duplicate spec/design content. Your value-add is audience translation, not restatement.
+8. If you encounter a cite that does not exist or has shifted lines, fix the cite in place and add a row to [27] noting the drift; do not silently change the brief.

Process:
1. Spot-check that every crates/, apps/, ios/ path:line cite in the new PLAN.md actually exists on master HEAD.
2. Spot-check that the status flags match reality (SHIPS = code exists, LANDED = design landed but code partial, PLANNED = no code).
3. Flag any doctrine vocabulary drift vs docs/product-spec/doctrine.md.
4. Note any milestone-status mismatches vs docs/plan/status.md or docs/perf/orchestration-log.md.
5. For each finding, classify FIX or REPORT per the rules above.
6. Apply all FIX-class changes (this is doc-only). Commit + push.
7. Emit a final REPORT section listing: (a) what you fixed (with commit SHA), (b) what needs orchestrator follow-up (with file:line + suggested approach).

Be terse. If clean, say so.

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# Cardinal Doctrines D0–D8
     2	
     3	[Back to Product Spec: Overview And Developer Experience](./overview-and-dx.md)
     4	
     5	Nine named principles that subsume the rest of the spec. Every API decision answers to at least one of these; conflicts between them resolve in the order listed.
     6	
     7	**Two kinds of doctrine.** D0–D5 are *policy* doctrines — they govern user-facing semantics (what the framework promises, what it forbids). D6–D8 are *substrate invariants* — they govern how the runtime is allowed to be implemented (what crosses FFI, how state changes propagate, what the hot path can do). Both kinds are equally binding; their distinction is the kind of review they enforce. Policy review flags "this API choice violates a user-facing principle"; substrate review flags "this implementation choice will leak across FFI / hide policy on the native side / degrade reactivity."
     8	
     9	## D0. Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`
    10	
    11	Per ADR-0009, NMP is a Nostr-native app kernel plus extension modules. The kernel provides substrate; protocol modules and app modules contribute typed variants via `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `DomainModule`, `CapabilityModule`, and `IdentityModule`. If implementing a real app requires adding domain nouns to `nmp-core`, the kernel boundary is wrong and must change.
    12	
    13	This rules out:
    14	
    15	- `nmp-core` becoming a junk drawer of every consumer's domain concepts.
    16	- App-specific business logic in Swift, Kotlin, or TypeScript shells.
    17	- Closed FFI enums that prevent modules from contributing typed views, actions, updates, capabilities, or identity scopes.
    18	
    19	Internal test-facing surface (e.g., `spawn_actor`) is gated behind `#[cfg(any(test, feature = "test-support"))]` so production builds export no actor internals.
    20	
    21	## D1. Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place
    22	
    23	Apps built with this framework **never withhold cached data and never block on fetches**. Every view payload field carries a value, not a "loading" status. Missing display names default to a shortened npub; missing pictures default to a deterministic identicon URI; missing timestamps default to "now". When a more authoritative value (e.g., the author's kind:0) arrives later, the view payload updates in place and the affected cell re-renders. The UI never sees a spinner gating already-renderable content.
    24	
    25	The doctrine is enforced by the view payload **types**: display fields are non-`Option`, placeholders are part of the type contract, and freshness is exposed (when relevant) as an optional badge hint, not a render gate. There is no `if has_profile { render } else { spinner }` pattern available in the API — the framework does not provide one.
    26	
    27	This rules out, by construction, the most common Nostr-client failure modes:
    28	
    29	- Hiding a post because the author's profile hasn't loaded yet.
    30	- Replacing cached profile metadata with a spinner because "we might have something newer."
    31	- Refusing to render threads because the root event isn't in cache.
    32	- Profile-picture flicker between cached and placeholder.
    33	
    34	## D2. Negentropy first, REQ second
    35	
    36	NIP-77 negentropy reconciliation is the default backfill mechanism. Every `(filter, relay)` pair the app touches is treated as a tracked sync target with a watermark. Live REQ remains the tailing path, but historical gaps consult coverage first and prefer sync over REQ scans when relays support it.
    37	
    38	This is not a product feature you opt into later; it is a subscription policy built on explicit coverage metadata. See §7.8.
    39	
    40	## D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
    41	
    42	Per NIP-65, reads and writes are routed to the relevant relays by framework policy without normal app code specifying them. Subscriptions with `authors` filters route to those authors' write relays; publishes go to the author's write relays plus tagged recipients' inbox relays; discovery falls back to a configurable indexer set.
    43	
    44	The safe public path does not ask the developer to pick relays per operation. Explicit override and diagnostic/test paths exist, but they are named, observable, and excluded from the default app-building flow.
    45	
    46	This rules out, by construction:
    47	
    48	- Posts to relays the author hasn't declared as write relays.
    49	- DMs leaked to public relays.
    50	- Silent reads against a default relay set that miss an author's actual relays; unknown relay lists surface as coverage/diagnostic state and use a bounded fallback policy.
    51	- Hand-rolled fan-out logic in app code.
    52	
    53	## D4. Single writer per fact; caches derive
    54	
    55	The "single source of truth" doctrine does not mean one cache — there are five layers (durable event store, in-memory working set, view payloads, gossip cache, platform reactive shadow). It means **one writer per fact**, and every downstream cache derives from the writer mechanically. Cache invalidation is not a concept in the public API. Recomputation happens in the actor; the platform receives new derived state.
    56	
    57	## D5. Snapshots bounded by what's open
    58	
    59	What crosses FFI is the projection through currently-open views, not the underlying event store. `AppState` carries small screen-shaped data plus a map of `ViewId → ViewPayload` for views currently in use. Closing a view evicts its payload from the snapshot. The event store itself never crosses FFI. See §6.2 and the FFI architecture appendix (§A1).
    60	
    61	## D6. Errors never cross FFI as exceptions
    62	
    63	Operational failures surface as `toast: Option<String>` state fields, never as exceptions or `Result<T, E>` across the FFI boundary. Long-running operations expose `busy` flags that clear on completion regardless of outcome. Native `dispatch` calls never need `try` / `catch`; native reconciler callbacks never receive a Rust error type.
    64	
    65	This rules out, by construction:
    66	
    67	- Swift / Kotlin code wrapping framework calls in `do { try }` or `try { } catch`.
    68	- Per-operation error-type proliferation across UniFFI.
    69	- Silent failure: every error has at least one observable state field carrying its consequences (a toast, a `busy` flag clearing, a diagnostic record per ADR-0007).
    70	
    71	Per RMP bible invariant #2 (`docs/aim.md` §2).
    72	
    73	## D7. Capabilities report; never decide policy
    74	
    75	Native bridges (Keychain, NIP-46 bunker, BGTask scheduler, `AVPlayer`, NIP-07 web extension, FilePicker, Blossom upload, etc.) execute platform APIs and report raw events back into the kernel. They never decide *whether to retry*, *whether an error is recoverable*, *which relay to publish to*, *which encryption scheme to negotiate*, *what state should become*, or *whether a duplicate request is a no-op*. Policy is Rust's; capabilities are reports.
    76	
    77	This rules out, by construction:
    78	
    79	- Capability bridges holding cached state beyond transient OS handles (Keychain handle, audio session token, network monitor, push registration).
    80	- Native code making decisions that the kernel should reproduce identically across platforms.
    81	- Capability lifecycles that aren't idempotent: start / stop / restart of any bridge must be safe N times.
    82	
    83	Per RMP bible cardinal rule #6 (`docs/aim.md` §2) and idempotence invariant #7.
    84	
    85	## D8. Reactivity contract: composite reverse index · ≤60 Hz/view · working-set bounded
    86	
    87	The substrate that delivers D1, D2, D4, D5 in practice. Every inserted event participates in a composite reverse index that names the views interested in it; view recompute is bounded to ≤60 deltas per second per view; the working set is hot-resident with claim-pinned overlays. Allocations after warmup are linear in active-view count, never in cached-event count. False-wakeup rate ≤ 0.10 candidates per delta.
    88	
    89	The idle-tick emit path is gated on `kernel.changed_since_emit()` — idle ticks that produce no state change must not emit snapshots (D8 regression guard).
    90	
    91	This rules out, by construction:
    92	
    93	- Per-event allocations on the hot path after warmup.
    94	- Wakeups proportional to event volume instead of view count.
    95	- Memory growth that tracks history depth instead of working set.
    96	- A view re-rendering at greater than 60 Hz regardless of upstream burst.
    97	
    98	Per ADRs 0001 (composite dependency keys), 0002 (per-view delta budget), 0003 (working-set memory), and 0004 (allocation measurement), all in `docs/decisions/`. Validated continuously by `reactivity-bench` (`crates/nmp-testing/bin/reactivity-bench/`).

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# Status — Where we are right now
     2	
     3	> Part of the [Build & Validation Plan](../plan.md). Kept fresh by the milestone heartbeat.
     4	
     5	Honest accounting before forecasting forward.
     6	
     7	## Implemented and running
     8	
     9	- **Kernel substrate** in `crates/nmp-core` (~3,800 LOC): actor on a dedicated OS thread, mailbox-driven (ADR feedback adopted — relay reads happen in tokio reader tasks, the actor blocks on its own channel with deadline timeouts), substrate trait families (`DomainModule`, `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `CapabilityModule`, `IdentityModule`) in `nmp-core/src/substrate/`, ingest pipeline (`kernel/ingest.rs`), claim/release refcounting for profile interest (commit `23ae829`), composite reverse-index dependency tracking.
    10	- **Live Nostr-connected iOS app** in `ios/NmpStress` (~1,375 LOC Swift): SwiftUI shell wired to the Rust kernel via raw C FFI. Connects to `wss://relay.primal.net` (content) + `wss://purplepag.es` (indexer). Renders seed-driven timeline from union of pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55 follow lists. Profile resolution with placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival per doctrine D1. Thread view. Diagnostics screen showing relay status, logical interests, wire subscriptions (ADR-0007).
    11	- **Fixture proving the kernel boundary** in `crates/fixture-todo-core` (~304 LOC) plus generated `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture`: a non-Nostr TODO module implementing all five substrate trait families, with codegen producing the per-app crate. Proves the kernel works for arbitrary domains, not just Nostr.
    12	- **Codegen tool** in `crates/nmp-codegen` (~423 LOC): reads `nmp.toml`, produces a per-app crate, has determinism tests.
    13	- **Benches** in `crates/nmp-testing`: `reactivity-bench` (composite-key reverse index + coalescer + working set; run 002 passed all ADR-0001..0004 gates) and `firehose-bench` (replay + capture + live modes; replay scenarios pass the modeled budget contract).
    14	- **Perf reports** in `docs/perf/` documenting reactivity-bench run 002, firehose-bench replay runs, and three iOS measurement reports (relay lifecycle, profile/thread subscriptions, the primal slice baseline).
    15	- **Architecture decisions** locked in 10 ADRs (`docs/decisions/0001`–`0010`).
    16	
    17	## Designed but not implemented
    18	
    19	- LMDB / IndexedDB persistent storage (in-memory only today).
    20	- NIP-65 outbox routing (hardcoded content + indexer relays today).
    21	- NIP-77 negentropy sync.
    22	- NIP-42 relay auth.
    23	- Multi-account / multi-session model and account switching.
    24	- Signer trait + local-key signer + NIP-46 bunker signer.
    25	- Action ledger + write path (compose / react / repost / quote).
    26	- NIP-17 messaging and the NSE companion crate.
    27	- Blossom uploads / downloads with resumable progress.
    28	- Wallet stack (NWC, NIP-57 zaps, Cashu, nutzaps).
    29	- Web-of-Trust subsystem.
    30	- UniFFI bindings (current iOS bridge is raw C FFI).
    31	- Android shell, Desktop shell, Web shell.
    32	- The `nmp` CLI scaffolding tool.
    33	- A non-Nostr-shaped product (podcast app) demonstrating the kernel boundary in production.
    34	
    35	## Gaps in the prior plan that this rewrite addresses
    36	
    37	- The prior plan was phase-numbered (Phase 1, 2, …) without explicit *demoable products* per phase.
    38	- NIP-42 wasn't covered.
    39	- Subscription compilation (the load-bearing NDK/Applesauce lesson) wasn't elevated as its own milestone.
    40	- Blossom and media-capability lifecycle (long-running, resumable, background) were one bullet under Phase 6.
    41	- No milestone proved the kernel boundary for a fundamentally non-social product.
    42	- The plan didn't reflect that M0 and M1 are largely done (M1 is now ✅ DONE — two exit-gate items deferred to M10.5 and M14 per T22; see m1-twitter-slice.md §Deferred).
    43	- **No dedicated FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof gate before the kernel-boundary proof.** The prior M11 implicitly assumed the FFI surface was ready; this rewrite makes it a separate milestone (M10.5).
    44	- **M11 was generic.** This rewrite ties it concretely to `/Users/pablofernandez/src/podcast` (the fully-functional Swift app) as the rebuild target, with copy-first UI fidelity and an explicit view-by-view module mapping.
    45	
    46	The plan below is a single ladder of eighteen milestones (M0–M17, with M10.5 inserted as the FFI gate), each producing a runnable artifact, ordered so that each milestone strictly adds capabilities to the prior demoable product.

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# NMP Builder Guide — PLAN
     2	
     3	> **Plan only.** TOC + per-section briefs. Parallel writer agents will produce `NN-*.md`. No prose docs here. Two audiences: **builders** (humans coming from NDK / Applesauce / raw `nostr-sdk`) and **agents** (LLMs extending NMP). Authoring rules: each file ≤ 300 LOC (AGENTS.md); cross-links `[NN — title](NN-name.md)`; cite `path:line` only if file exists today on master; distill not duplicate; ≥3 anti-patterns and ≥2 concrete deliverables per section.

   266	## Writer-agent process
   267	
   268	1. Read this PLAN + your section's brief + cited prereqs (briefs or drafted sections) before drafting.
   269	2. Honor the `status` field strictly. SHIPS may cite `crates/`/`apps/`/`ios/` `path:line`; LANDED cites design + ADRs + partial code; PLANNED cites plan files + ADRs only.

   271	4. Keep your file ≤ 300 LOC. If exceeded, split per the brief's hint (e.g. 05a/05b) and update the TOC in a follow-up.
   272	5. Include ≥3 anti-patterns and ≥2 concrete deliverables from the brief.
   273	6. End every section with `See also:` listing `xref` exactly as `[NN — title](NN-name.md)`.
   274	7. Distill — do not duplicate spec/design content. Your value-add is audience translation, not restatement.
   275	8. If you encounter a cite that does not exist or has shifted lines, fix the cite in place and add a row to [27] noting the drift; do not silently change the brief.

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba docs/plan.md | sed -n '1,120p'" in /Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/.claude/worktrees/agent-a1a6c8d868b9ac3f0
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# Build & Validation Plan
     2	
     3	> Companion to `docs/product-spec.md` (what we ship) and the design docs in `docs/design/` (how each subsystem works). This document defines **the single ladder of milestones**, each one a runnable product that proves a specific architectural claim with real (not modeled) evidence.
     4	
     5	> **Four arcs:** Kernel substrate + Nostr social stack (M0–M10) → FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof (M10.5) → kernel-boundary proof with a non-social-domain app (M11, the **`../podcast` rebuild on NMP**) → WoT + cross-platform + release (M13–M17; M12 Wallet deferred post-v1 per [scope-adjustments-2026-05-18](plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md)).
     6	
     7	> **Each milestone is gated.** Every milestone ends with: a runnable artifact, automated tests in `nmp-testing`, a measured-numbers report in `docs/perf/m<N>/`, and an explicit ADR if a design decision was revised in flight. **No silent endings.** **No "for later" carve-outs** — if a slice is in the milestone scope, it ships in that milestone, or the milestone is not done.
     8	
     9	> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5, D0–D8): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 negentropy first, REQ second · D3 outbox routing automatic, manual relay is the opt-out · D4 single writer per fact; caches derive · D5 snapshots bounded by open views · D6 errors never cross FFI as exceptions · D7 capabilities report, never decide policy · D8 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60 Hz/view, working-set bounded). Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
    10	
    11	---
    12	
    13	## Supporting sections
    14	
    15	- [Status — where we are right now](plan/status.md)
    16	- [Principles of execution](plan/principles.md)
    17	- [Subsystem coverage matrix + NIP roadmap](plan/subsystem-matrix.md)
    18	- [Parallelization opportunities](plan/parallelization.md)
    19	- [Test pyramid](plan/test-pyramid.md)
    20	- [CI / pre-merge hygiene](plan/ci-hygiene.md)
    21	- [Decision log](plan/decision-log.md)
    22	- [Scope adjustments 2026-05-18](plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md) — M9 DMs + M12 Wallet deferred; M11.5 Highlighter added; framework-magic contract
    23	- [Post-v1 milestones](plan/post-v1.md) — deferred M9 (DMs) and M12 (Wallet)
    24	
    25	---
    26	
    27	## The milestone ladder
    28	
    29	Each milestone has: **demo product**, **scope (what gets built)**, **subsystem deliverables**, **exit gate (measurable)**, and **runnable artifact**. Estimates are for one experienced developer focused on the work; they are not commitments.
    30	
    31	| Milestone | Title | Arc | Status |
    32	|---|---|---|---|
    33	| [M0](plan/m0-fixture.md) | Kernel substrate + non-Nostr fixture | Arc 1 — Social stack | ✅ DONE |
    34	| [M1](plan/m1-twitter-slice.md) | Read-only Twitter slice on iOS | Arc 1 — Social stack | 🟡 Largely done |
    35	| [M2](plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md) | Subscription compilation + outbox routing + kind:3 auto-tracking | Arc 1 — Social stack | |
    36	| [M3](plan/m3-persistence.md) | Persistence (LMDB) + full insert invariants | Arc 1 — Social stack | |
    37	| [M4](plan/m4-negentropy.md) | NIP-77 negentropy sync engine | Arc 1 — Social stack | |
    38	| [M5](plan/m5-nip42.md) | NIP-42 auth | Arc 1 — Social stack | |
    39	| [M6](plan/m6-signers-write.md) | Sessions + signers (incl. bunker:// + nsec creation) + write path | Arc 1 — Social stack | |
    40	| [M7](plan/m7-interaction-loop.md) | Reactions + Thread + Reply | Arc 1 — Social stack | |
    41	| [M8](plan/m8-multi-account.md) | Multi-session (multi-account) clients | Arc 1 — Social stack | |
    42	| ~~M9~~ | ~~NIP-17 DMs + NSE~~ | ~~Arc 1~~ | [deferred post-v1](plan/post-v1.md) |
    43	| [M10](plan/m10-blossom.md) | Blossom + media + long-running capabilities | Arc 1 — Social stack | |
    44	| [M10.5](plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md) | FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof | Hard gate | |
    45	| [M11](plan/m11-podcast.md) | Podcast app — kernel-boundary proof | Arc 2 — Kernel boundary | |
    46	| [M11.5](plan/m11.5-highlighter.md) | Highlighter rebuild + `nmp-nip29` crate | Arc 2 — Kernel boundary | |
    47	| ~~M12~~ | ~~Wallet (NWC + zaps + Cashu + nutzaps)~~ | ~~Arc 3~~ | [deferred post-v1](plan/post-v1.md) |
    48	| [M13](plan/m13-wot.md) | Web-of-Trust | Arc 3 — Release | |
    49	| [M14](plan/m14-uniffi.md) | UniFFI migration | Arc 3 — Release | |
    50	| [M15](plan/m15-cross-platform.md) | Cross-platform: Android + Desktop + Web | Arc 3 — Release | |
    51	| [M16](plan/m16-cli-starter.md) | CLI + starter app + recipe book | Arc 3 — Release | |
    52	| [M17](plan/m17-release.md) | v1 release | Arc 3 — Release | |
    53	
    54	---
    55	
    56	## What this plan is not
    57	
    58	- **Not a schedule.** No dates, no person-months. Milestones are sequential; their durations depend on team size and surface complexity. Estimates per milestone are guidance only.
    59	- **Not a marketing roadmap.** v1 ships when M17 gates are met, not on a calendar.
    60	- **Not exhaustive about post-v1 work.** NIP-23 long-form, NIP-71 video, and additional protocol modules are post-v1. NIP-29 groups ship in v1 via [M11.5](plan/m11.5-highlighter.md) (Highlighter rebuild). Additional app demonstrations (TENEX-lite, etc.) are post-v1.
    61	- **Not silent about gaps.** The [status doc](plan/status.md) names exactly what is and isn't built. As the ladder progresses, the status doc gets revised so the plan stays honest about state.
    62	
    63	The plan exists so that any single milestone can be picked up cold by someone reading this doc + `product-spec.md` + the relevant ADRs and design docs, and they can execute without bothering the rest of the team.

 succeeded in 0ms:
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:27:## 1.5 Cardinal doctrines D0–D8
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:29:See [`docs/product-spec/doctrine.md`](./doctrine.md) for the full text of all nine doctrines.
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:31:Summary: D0–D5 are *policy* doctrines (user-facing semantics); D6–D8 are *substrate invariants* (runtime implementation constraints). Both are equally binding. Every API decision answers to at least one; conflicts resolve in the order D0 > D1 > … > D8.
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:35:| D0 | No app nouns in `nmp-core`; test surface gated behind `test-support` feature | Policy |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:36:| D1 | Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place | Policy |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:37:| D2 | Negentropy first, REQ second | Policy |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:38:| D3 | Outbox routing automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out | Policy |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:39:| D4 | Single writer per fact; caches derive | Policy |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:40:| D5 | Snapshots bounded by what's open | Policy |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:41:| D6 | Errors never cross FFI as exceptions | Substrate |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:42:| D7 | Capabilities report; never decide policy | Substrate |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:43:| D8 | Reactivity contract: composite reverse index · ≤60 Hz/view · working-set bounded; idle-tick emit gated on `changed_since_emit()` | Substrate |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:132:A scripted action sequence (defined in `crates/nmp-testing`) run against the starter app on all four platforms produces byte-identical `AppState` JSON snapshots after each action. Divergence is a framework defect, not a platform issue.
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:153:| `nmp-nip65` | Mailboxes view and outbox routing helpers | Pure Rust |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:193:- Background sync via NIP-77 negentropy on foreground.
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:199:The proof app is the substrate for cross-platform consistency tests (§3.5): the same scripted action sequence runs against the proof app on all four platforms and `AppState` JSON snapshots must match.
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:261:- Pick relays per subscription or publish (outbox handles it).
README.md:15:- **M2 (subscription compilation + outbox + NIP-65):** ✅ planner + 5-case partition + Rule 9 group-id merge on master. 5 rounds of codex follow-up landed. Address-pointer support.
README.md:16:- **M3 (LMDB + insert invariants + claim GC):** ✅ EventStore trait + MemEventStore (sole writer per D4) + LmdbEventStore skeleton + verify_and_persist outcome gating for kind:0/3/10002 + kernel/mod.rs split 561→179 + kernel/ingest.rs split into 5 files (`83a964e`). 38 nmp-core tests passing.
README.md:17:- **M4 (NIP-77 negentropy):** ✅ LANDED (`076173d` — `crates/nmp-nip77/` reconciler + wire + capability + capability_domain).
README.md:27:- **Framework-magic contract** (kind:3 auto-tracking, `bunker://` onboarding, new-nsec creation, outbox-by-default-on-publish): designed at [`docs/design/framework-magic.md`](docs/design/framework-magic.md). 14 tests in `crates/nmp-testing/tests/framework_magic_contract.rs` — **7 of 14 passing on master; 7 ignored pending M2 (C5/C8/C13), M4 (C10), M6 (C7/C11), M8 (C12)**.
README.md:31:- **Research foundation:** NDK + Applesauce deep-dives at [`docs/research/ndk/`](docs/research/ndk/) and [`docs/research/applesauce/`](docs/research/applesauce/) — outbox routing, kind:3 auto-tracking gap, subscription compilation, signers, gotchas, missing features.
README.md:35:- **Cardinal doctrine (D0–D8).** Canonical wording from [`docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5](docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md). Every PR is reviewed against this rubric. A change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected. Conflicts resolve in the order listed. **Two kinds:** D0–D5 are *policy* doctrines (user-facing semantics); D6–D8 are *substrate invariants* (runtime / FFI / hot-path constraints). Both equally binding.
README.md:36:  - **D0** Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`.
README.md:37:  - **D1** Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place. Placeholders are part of the type contract.
README.md:38:  - **D2** Negentropy first, REQ second. NIP-77 reconciliation with durable watermarks is the default backfill. (M4.)
README.md:39:  - **D3** Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out. (M2.)
README.md:40:  - **D4** Single writer per fact; caches derive. Cache invalidation is not a concept in the public API.
README.md:41:  - **D5** Snapshots bounded by what's open. `AppState` carries the projection through currently-open views.
README.md:42:  - **D6** Errors never cross FFI as exceptions. Surface as `toast: Option<String>` state fields. (RMP bible invariant #2.)
README.md:43:  - **D7** Capabilities report; never decide policy. Native bridges execute platform APIs; Rust decides retry/recovery/routing. (RMP bible rule #6.)
README.md:44:  - **D8** Reactivity contract: composite reverse index · ≤60 Hz/view · working-set bounded · zero per-event allocations after warmup. Validated by `reactivity-bench`. (ADR-0001..0004.)
README.md:50:- **Post-merge codex review.** After every push to master, `codex exec` reviews the diff against the doctrine + file-size rules + spec coherence. Codex output saved to `docs/perf/codex-reviews/<sha>.md`. Any real concerns become fix-it TaskList entries.
README.md:90:- **Codex review on every merge.** `codex exec` is the post-merge doctrine reviewer. Saved transcripts in `docs/perf/codex-reviews/`.
README.md:101:| `docs/product-spec.md` + `docs/product-spec/` | What we ship at v1. The cardinal doctrine D0–D8 lives in §1.5. |
README.md:104:| `docs/research/` | Reverse-engineering notes on NDK + Applesauce — outbox, kind:3 auto-tracking, signers, gotchas, missing-features deltas. |

docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5, D0–D8): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 negentropy first, REQ second · D3 outbox routing automatic, manual relay is the opt-out · D4 single writer per fact; caches derive · D5 snapshots bounded by open views · D6 errors never cross FFI as exceptions · D7 capabilities report, never decide policy · D8 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60 Hz/view, working-set bounded). Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/plan.md:35:| [M2](plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md) | Subscription compilation + outbox routing + kind:3 auto-tracking | Arc 1 — Social stack | |
docs/plan.md:37:| [M4](plan/m4-negentropy.md) | NIP-77 negentropy sync engine | Arc 1 — Social stack | |
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:1:# Cardinal Doctrines D0–D8
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:7:**Two kinds of doctrine.** D0–D5 are *policy* doctrines — they govern user-facing semantics (what the framework promises, what it forbids). D6–D8 are *substrate invariants* — they govern how the runtime is allowed to be implemented (what crosses FFI, how state changes propagate, what the hot path can do). Both kinds are equally binding; their distinction is the kind of review they enforce. Policy review flags "this API choice violates a user-facing principle"; substrate review flags "this implementation choice will leak across FFI / hide policy on the native side / degrade reactivity."
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:9:## D0. Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:21:## D1. Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:25:The doctrine is enforced by the view payload **types**: display fields are non-`Option`, placeholders are part of the type contract, and freshness is exposed (when relevant) as an optional badge hint, not a render gate. There is no `if has_profile { render } else { spinner }` pattern available in the API — the framework does not provide one.
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:34:## D2. Negentropy first, REQ second
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:36:NIP-77 negentropy reconciliation is the default backfill mechanism. Every `(filter, relay)` pair the app touches is treated as a tracked sync target with a watermark. Live REQ remains the tailing path, but historical gaps consult coverage first and prefer sync over REQ scans when relays support it.
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:40:## D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:53:## D4. Single writer per fact; caches derive
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:55:The "single source of truth" doctrine does not mean one cache — there are five layers (durable event store, in-memory working set, view payloads, gossip cache, platform reactive shadow). It means **one writer per fact**, and every downstream cache derives from the writer mechanically. Cache invalidation is not a concept in the public API. Recomputation happens in the actor; the platform receives new derived state.
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:57:## D5. Snapshots bounded by what's open
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:61:## D6. Errors never cross FFI as exceptions
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:63:Operational failures surface as `toast: Option<String>` state fields, never as exceptions or `Result<T, E>` across the FFI boundary. Long-running operations expose `busy` flags that clear on completion regardless of outcome. Native `dispatch` calls never need `try` / `catch`; native reconciler callbacks never receive a Rust error type.
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:73:## D7. Capabilities report; never decide policy
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:75:Native bridges (Keychain, NIP-46 bunker, BGTask scheduler, `AVPlayer`, NIP-07 web extension, FilePicker, Blossom upload, etc.) execute platform APIs and report raw events back into the kernel. They never decide *whether to retry*, *whether an error is recoverable*, *which relay to publish to*, *which encryption scheme to negotiate*, *what state should become*, or *whether a duplicate request is a no-op*. Policy is Rust's; capabilities are reports.
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:85:## D8. Reactivity contract: composite reverse index · ≤60 Hz/view · working-set bounded
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:87:The substrate that delivers D1, D2, D4, D5 in practice. Every inserted event participates in a composite reverse index that names the views interested in it; view recompute is bounded to ≤60 deltas per second per view; the working set is hot-resident with claim-pinned overlays. Allocations after warmup are linear in active-view count, never in cached-event count. False-wakeup rate ≤ 0.10 candidates per delta.
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:89:The idle-tick emit path is gated on `kernel.changed_since_emit()` — idle ticks that produce no state change must not emit snapshots (D8 regression guard).
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:96:- A view re-rendering at greater than 60 Hz regardless of upstream burst.

 succeeded in 0ms:

docs/builder-guide/PLAN.md:186:- covers: end-to-end build of a kind:1 microblog: `nmp.toml`, app-core crate (one DomainModule + one ViewModule + one ActionModule wired to publish engine), regenerate, wire SwiftUI, run on simulator. Mirrors `fixture-todo-core` structurally and references `apps/podcast/podcast-core` for a real "non-fixture" app crate. Today's wiring uses raw C FFI; UniFFI path is M14.
docs/builder-guide/PLAN.md:188:- deliver: complete file tree of the example; per-file ~30-line skeletons; build/run cheatsheet (`cargo build`, codegen invocation, iOS sim run); "what publishes today vs tomorrow" milestone matrix (publish substrate ships today, multi-account M8, UniFFI M14).
docs/builder-guide/PLAN.md:202:- deliver: C1–C13 table reproduced with "what the app gets" column added; status column ([DONE]/[PARTIAL]/[PENDING M_n]); mini-recipe for adding C14 (ADR template + test name convention).
docs/builder-guide/PLAN.md:217:- anti: inventing terms not present in the codebase; re-defining a term differently per section; conflating ViewSpec (input) with ViewPayload (output); using "session" and "account" interchangeably; conflating M8-subs and M8-multi-account.
docs/builder-guide/PLAN.md:242:- covers: a running list of places where docs claim more than code delivers today: e.g. spec talks about full UniFFI `AppUpdate::ViewBatch` but the live FFI emits a single JSON snapshot; `nmp init` CLI is M16 (not built); UniFFI is M14 (not built); the iOS shells `NmpPodcast` + `NmpHighlighter` are skeletons (vs `NmpStress` 1,375 LOC live); M9 DMs + M12 Wallet are post-v1 deferrals; the actor still uses constants `relay.primal.net` + `purplepag.es` even though the planner can route to mailboxes (wiring gap). Each entry: claim · evidence · status · owning milestone · severity.
docs/builder-guide/PLAN.md:243:- cite: `docs/product-spec/api-surface.md` §6.1, §6.4 (claimed shape); `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs:44-310` (actual surface today); `crates/nmp-core/src/app.rs:1-30` (actual KernelUpdate); `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs:1-50` (hardcoded relays); `docs/plan/m16-cli-starter.md`; `docs/plan/m14-uniffi.md`; `docs/plan/m15-cross-platform.md`; `docs/plan/post-v1.md`; `docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md`.
docs/builder-guide/PLAN.md:262:Shipping crates → sections: `nmp-core` (substrate/planner/store/subs/publish) → 02, 04–12, 14, 22; `nmp-signers` → 11; `nmp-nip29` (reference protocol module) → 20; `nmp-nip42` → 14; `nmp-nip77` → 13; `nmp-codegen` → 15; `nmp-testing` → 18; `fixture-todo-core` → 05, 19; `apps/podcast/*` → 02, 16, 19; `nmp-highlighter-core` (M11.5 step 0 stub) → 20; `ios/NmpStress` → 17; `ios/{NmpPodcast,NmpHighlighter}` skeletons → 17, 27.
docs/builder-guide/PLAN.md:264:Milestone status → sections: ✅ M0 → 02,05,15,18,19; ✅ M1 → 17,26; ✅ M2 → 07,10,21; ✅ M3 → 08,09; ✅ M4 → 13; ✅ M5 → 14; ✅ M6 → 11,12; ✅ M7 (publishing + interaction loop) → 05,12,21; ✅ M8 (multi-account + M8-subs lifecycle) → 11,14; ✅ M10.5 → 15,17,18; ✅ M11 (podcast scaffold) → 02,16,19; ✅ M11.5 (Highlighter step 0 + nmp-nip29) → 20,27; ⌛ M10 Blossom → 27; ⌛ M13 WoT → 27; ⌛ M14 UniFFI → 15,27; ⌛ M15 cross-platform → 15,17,27; ⌛ M16 CLI/starter → 19,26,27; ⌛ M17 release → 22,27; ❌ M9 DMs + M12 Wallet (post-v1) → 01,27.

README.md:13:- **M0 (kernel substrate + non-Nostr fixture):** ✅ DONE.
README.md:14:- **M1 (read-only Twitter slice on live iOS):** ✅ DONE (`701d0e5` — firehose-bench `live` cold_start 258ms first-item / 3587ms filled-timeline; profile_thrashing 0 leaked subs).
README.md:15:- **M2 (subscription compilation + outbox + NIP-65):** ✅ planner + 5-case partition + Rule 9 group-id merge on master. 5 rounds of codex follow-up landed. Address-pointer support.
README.md:16:- **M3 (LMDB + insert invariants + claim GC):** ✅ EventStore trait + MemEventStore (sole writer per D4) + LmdbEventStore skeleton + verify_and_persist outcome gating for kind:0/3/10002 + kernel/mod.rs split 561→179 + kernel/ingest.rs split into 5 files (`83a964e`). 38 nmp-core tests passing.
README.md:17:- **M4 (NIP-77 negentropy):** ✅ LANDED (`076173d` — `crates/nmp-nip77/` reconciler + wire + capability + capability_domain).
README.md:18:- **M5 (NIP-42 relay auth):** ✅ LANDED (`e69c3a4` — `crates/nmp-nip42/` state + parsers + builder + driver).
README.md:19:- **M6 (sessions + signers):** ✅ LANDED (`9944bed` — `crates/nmp-signers/` with Signer trait + Local/Nip46/Nip07 + AccountManager + kind:3 auto-rewire). ADR-0015 captures design. Sessions research synthesis at `docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md` informed the design (AccountPublic/AccountSecret split, signer-mismatch post-conditions, bunker:// strict-hex parser).
README.md:20:- **M7 (publishing pipeline):** ✅ LANDED (`08fc01f` — Publish action + per-relay state machine + NIP-65 auto-route + durable retry queue + PublishStatusView).
README.md:21:- **M8 (subscription lifecycle / RelayManager):** ✅ LANDED (`9ca90c9` — registry + trigger inbox + wire emitter + connection pool + auth gate).
README.md:22:- **M10 (Blossom + long-running capabilities):** pending.
README.md:23:- **M10.5 (FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof):** S2 6/6 + S3 6/6 + S4 6/6 + S5 5/5 PASS with VerifiedEvent injection. Round-4 in flight (`m105-round4-fixes`). **Hard gate before M11 iOS deploy.**
README.md:24:- **M11 (rebuild of `/Users/pablofernandez/src/podcast` on NMP):** 🟡 Step 0 in flight (`m11-step0-impl` — Swift verbatim + 5 podcast crates scaffold; some files already landed in `apps/podcast/`).
README.md:25:- **M11.5 (rebuild of `/Users/pablofernandez/Work/hl/app` Highlighter + `nmp-nip29` crate):** ✅ Step 0 LANDED (`5178cfc` — full nmp-nip29 + nmp-highlighter-core + ios/NmpHighlighter Swift Sources verbatim + planner case_e_relay_pin + Rule 9 group-id merge).
README.md:26:- **M13 (WoT), M14 (UniFFI), M15 (cross-platform), M16 (CLI), M17 (v1 release):** scoped, pending.
README.md:27:- **Framework-magic contract** (kind:3 auto-tracking, `bunker://` onboarding, new-nsec creation, outbox-by-default-on-publish): designed at [`docs/design/framework-magic.md`](docs/design/framework-magic.md). 14 tests in `crates/nmp-testing/tests/framework_magic_contract.rs` — **7 of 14 passing on master; 7 ignored pending M2 (C5/C8/C13), M4 (C10), M6 (C7/C11), M8 (C12)**.
README.md:29:- **Sessions research** (`de9e7b4` + `a3bf036`): NDK + applesauce deep-dives + NMP M6 synthesis at `docs/research/sessions/`.
README.md:30:- **Plan structure:** `docs/plan.md` is the index; per-milestone files under `docs/plan/m*.md` (≤300 LOC each). Scope shifts captured in [`docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md`](docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md) — DMs (was M9) and Wallet (was M12) deferred to post-v1 (see [`docs/plan/post-v1.md`](docs/plan/post-v1.md)).
README.md:38:  - **D2** Negentropy first, REQ second. NIP-77 reconciliation with durable watermarks is the default backfill. (M4.)
README.md:39:  - **D3** Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out. (M2.)
README.md:47:- **DMs (was M9) and Wallet (was M12) deferred to post-v1.** Documented in `docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md`. M11.5 Highlighter takes their slot.
README.md:51:- **Empirical iOS proof before podcast rebuild (M10.5 gate).** No "we'll wire it up later" — the FFI surface ships rock-solid (stress harness + simulator-driven Sonnet-agent UI fleet + Instruments leaks audit + iPhone-12 baseline) before any line of M11 code is written.
README.md:99:| `docs/plan.md` + `docs/plan/` | Milestone ladder (M0–M17) with exit gates per milestone. |
README.md:108:| `ios/NmpStress` | iOS SwiftUI shell wired to the Rust kernel via raw C FFI (will migrate to UniFFI in M14). |
docs/plan/status.md:42:- The plan didn't reflect that M0 and M1 are largely done (M1 is now ✅ DONE — two exit-gate items deferred to M10.5 and M14 per T22; see m1-twitter-slice.md §Deferred).
docs/plan/status.md:43:- **No dedicated FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof gate before the kernel-boundary proof.** The prior M11 implicitly assumed the FFI surface was ready; this rewrite makes it a separate milestone (M10.5).
docs/plan/status.md:44:- **M11 was generic.** This rewrite ties it concretely to `/Users/pablofernandez/src/podcast` (the fully-functional Swift app) as the rebuild target, with copy-first UI fidelity and an explicit view-by-view module mapping.
docs/plan/status.md:46:The plan below is a single ladder of eighteen milestones (M0–M17, with M10.5 inserted as the FFI gate), each producing a runnable artifact, ordered so that each milestone strictly adds capabilities to the prior demoable product.

docs/design/framework-magic.md:11:## Section map
docs/design/framework-magic.md:14:- [Kind:3 auto-tracking — follow-list change recompiles dependent subscriptions](framework-magic/kind3.md) (C5)
docs/design/framework-magic.md:15:- [Replaceable & delete invariants — supersession, parameterized supersession, kind:5, NIP-40](framework-magic/replaceable.md) (C1–C4)
docs/design/framework-magic.md:16:- [Outbox routing — read fan-out, write fan-out, private events fail closed](framework-magic/outbox.md) (C6, C7)
docs/design/framework-magic.md:17:- [Subscriptions — dedup, coalesce, auto-close, buffered batches](framework-magic/subs.md) (C8)
docs/design/framework-magic.md:18:- [Sync & provenance — watermarks, NIP-77 backfill, redelivery merge](framework-magic/sync.md) (C9, C10)
docs/design/framework-magic.md:19:- [Signers & onboarding — bunker://, nsec creation, Keychain persistence](framework-magic/signers.md) (C11)
docs/design/framework-magic.md:20:- [Sessions — account switch = state, view rebuild without imperative dance](framework-magic/sessions.md) (C12)
docs/design/framework-magic.md:21:- [Capabilities & rendering — best-effort placeholders, in-place refinement](framework-magic/capabilities.md) (C13)
docs/design/framework-magic.md:22:- [Test scaffolding — the `framework_magic_contract.rs` harness, naming convention, coverage meta-test](framework-magic/test-scaffolding.md)
docs/design/framework-magic.md:24:## The 13 contract bullets
docs/design/framework-magic.md:26:Each row binds a behavior to: the sub-file that specifies it, the test name in `crates/nmp-testing/tests/framework_magic_contract.rs`, the milestone that owns the implementation, and the doctrine clause it discharges.
docs/design/framework-magic.md:30:| C1 | Replaceable-event supersession (kind 0 / 3 / 10000–19999) on insert | replaceable.md | `c1_replaceable_supersedes_on_insert` | **[DONE]** kernel | spec §7.1 row "Replaceable kinds"; §3.3 bug #1 |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:31:| C2 | Parameterized replaceable supersession (30000–39999) by `(pubkey, kind, d-tag)` | replaceable.md | `c2_parameterized_replaceable_supersedes_by_dtag` | **[PENDING M3]** | spec §7.1 row "Parameterized replaceable"; §3.3 bug #1 |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:32:| C3 | Kind:5 delete propagation: referenced events removed, tombstone persisted | replaceable.md | `c3_kind5_delete_removes_referenced_and_tombstones` | **[PENDING M3]** | spec §7.1 row "Kind 5 (delete)" |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:33:| C4 | NIP-40 expiration auto-removes event at expiry; survives actor restart | replaceable.md | `c4_nip40_expiration_removes_and_persists_schedule` | **[PENDING M3]** | spec §7.1 row "NIP-40 expiration" |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:34:| C5 | Kind:3 auto-tracking: active account's follow-list change recompiles dependent subscriptions transparently | kind3.md | `c5_kind3_change_recompiles_follow_dependent_subs` | **[PENDING M2]** | scope-adj §"Folded into M2"; D3; M2 design §4 (Trigger::Nip65Arrived analog for kind:3) |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:35:| C6 | Outbox read routing: `authors`-filter subscriptions fan out to those authors' write relays (NIP-65), de-duplicated | outbox.md | `c6_authors_subscription_routes_to_per_author_write_relays` | **[PENDING M2]** | D3; spec §7.3 row "Subscription with `authors`"; M2 design §7 |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:36:| C7 | Outbox write routing: publishes go to author write + `#p`-recipient inbox; private (gift-wrap) events fail closed when recipient inbox is unknown | outbox.md | `c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed` | **[PENDING M2 seam → M6 publish]** | D3; spec §7.3 rows "Publish*"; §3.3 bugs #3, #4 |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:37:| C8 | Subscription planner deduplicates overlapping interests into one wire REQ per relay, auto-closes on EOSE / last-consumer-drop, and buffers ingress to ≤60Hz per view | subs.md | `c8_subscriptions_coalesce_autoclose_and_buffer` | **[PENDING M2]** | spec §7.2; §3.3 bug #2, bug #8 |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:38:| C9 | Provenance preserved: same event id arriving from N relays merges into one stored event with N-entry provenance set; original `id` and signature untouched | sync.md | `c9_provenance_merges_across_relay_redeliveries` | **[PENDING M3]** | aim §6 doctrine 10; spec §7.1 row "Provenance"; §3.3 bug #10 |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:39:| C10 | Sync watermarks: planner consults `(filter, relay)` coverage before issuing historical REQ; full coverage makes cache-miss authoritative; NIP-77 negentropy is the default backfill where supported | sync.md | `c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss` | **[LANDED M4]** | D2; spec §7.1 watermarks, §7.8 sync engine |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:40:| C11 | Signer onboarding: pasted `bunker://` URL parses + connects via NIP-46; "create new nsec" generates, NIP-49-encrypts, and persists via KeyringCapability — both as kernel actions, no app code | signers.md | `c11_bunker_url_and_nsec_creation_complete_via_actions` | **[PENDING M6]** | scope-adj §"Folded into M6"; spec §7.4 |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:41:| C12 | Account switch is a state transition: dispatching the switch action re-resolves every `ActiveAccount`-scoped view without the app issuing CLOSE/REQ or rebuilding view handles | sessions.md | `c12_account_switch_rebinds_views_without_imperative_dance` | **[PENDING M8]** | D4; spec §7.4; §3.3 bug #5; M2 §4 trigger A4 |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:42:| C13 | Best-effort rendering: every view payload field is non-`Option`; missing data uses defined placeholders (shortened npub, identicon, "just now"); the same payload updates in place when authoritative data arrives | capabilities.md | `c13_view_payload_uses_placeholders_then_refines_in_place` | **[DONE]** in placeholder shape; **[PENDING M2/M3]** for in-place refinement on enrich | D1; spec §7.6 "Best-effort field contract"; aim §4.12 |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:44:**Bullet count:** 13 (eleven sourced verbatim from `scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md`; two — **C3** kind:5 delete propagation and **C4** NIP-40 expiration — derived from `product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.1 because they are guaranteed invariants of the same insert path and the contract is incomplete without them).
docs/design/framework-magic.md:46:**Test names** (13 behavior tests + 1 coverage meta-test = 14 total, all in `crates/nmp-testing/tests/framework_magic_contract.rs` — see [test-scaffolding.md](framework-magic/test-scaffolding.md) for the harness):
docs/design/framework-magic.md:61:14. `contract_surface_complete` — **meta-test**; asserts every behavior listed in this index has a corresponding `#[test] fn` in `framework_magic_contract.rs` (ignored or not). Drift between this doc and the test file fails the build.
docs/design/framework-magic.md:63:Tests for behaviors whose owning milestone has not landed are checked in with `#[ignore = "pending M_n"]`; the meta-test still counts them. This is the "doc says 13, code tests 11" regression the file-naming convention break (cross-cutting, not milestone-prefixed) is designed to support — see [test-scaffolding.md](framework-magic/test-scaffolding.md) §1.
docs/design/framework-magic.md:65:## How this contract evolves
docs/design/framework-magic.md:69:1. Which contract bullets transitioned from `[PENDING M_n]` to `[DONE]`.
docs/design/framework-magic.md:70:2. Which `#[ignore = "pending M_n"]` lines were removed from `framework_magic_contract.rs`.
docs/design/framework-magic.md:75:## Research citations (resolved)
docs/design/framework-magic.md:79:- `kind3.md` §3 — NDK's session layer opens a long-lived REQ for active-user events (including kind:3) at `sessions/src/store.ts:184-194`; kind:3 is processed in `handleContactListEvent` at `store.ts:492-512`, which updates `session.followSet`. Core NDK has **no** automatic open-subscription rewire on follow-list change; Svelte gets it via runes (`subscription.svelte.ts:164-177`), React requires explicit deps (`subscribe.ts:110`). NMP's kernel fills this gap with `Trigger::FollowListChanged` (C5).
docs/design/framework-magic.md:85:## Non-goals

    16	pub(crate) enum RelayRole {
    17	    Content,
    18	    Indexer,
    19	}
    20	
    21	impl RelayRole {
    22	    pub(crate) fn all() -> [Self; 2] {
    23	        [Self::Content, Self::Indexer]
    24	    }
    25	
    26	    pub(crate) fn key(self) -> &'static str {
    27	        match self {
    28	            Self::Content => "content",
    29	            Self::Indexer => "indexer",
    30	        }
    31	    }
    32	
    33	    pub(crate) fn url(self) -> &'static str {
    34	        match self {
    35	            Self::Content => CONTENT_RELAY_URL,
    36	            Self::Indexer => INDEXER_RELAY_URL,
    37	        }
    38	    }
    39	}

     1	use crate::relay::RelayRole;
     2	use std::collections::VecDeque;
     3	use std::net::TcpStream;
     4	use std::sync::mpsc::{self, Receiver, RecvTimeoutError, Sender, TryRecvError};
     5	use std::sync::Once;
     6	use std::thread;
     7	use std::time::{Duration, Instant};
     8	use tungstenite::stream::MaybeTlsStream;
     9	use tungstenite::{connect, Message, WebSocket};
    10	
    11	pub(crate) enum RelayEvent {
    12	    Connected {
    13	        role: RelayRole,
    14	        generation: u64,
    15	    },
    16	    Failed {
    17	        role: RelayRole,
    18	        generation: u64,
    19	        error: String,
    20	    },
    21	    Closed {
    22	        role: RelayRole,
    23	        generation: u64,
    24	    },
    25	    Message {
    26	        role: RelayRole,
    27	        generation: u64,
    28	        message: Message,
    29	    },
    30	}
    31	
    32	impl RelayEvent {
    33	    pub(crate) fn role(&self) -> RelayRole {
    34	        match self {
    35	            Self::Connected { role, .. }
    36	            | Self::Failed { role, .. }
    37	            | Self::Closed { role, .. }
    38	            | Self::Message { role, .. } => *role,
    39	        }
    40	    }
    41	
    42	    pub(crate) fn generation(&self) -> u64 {
    43	        match self {
    44	            Self::Connected { generation, .. }
    45	            | Self::Failed { generation, .. }
    46	            | Self::Closed { generation, .. }
    47	            | Self::Message { generation, .. } => *generation,
    48	        }
    49	    }
    50	}
    51	
    52	pub(crate) enum RelayCommand {
    53	    Send(String),
    54	    Shutdown,
    55	}
    56	
    57	enum RelayWorkerResult {
    58	    Reconnect,
    59	    Shutdown,
    60	}
    61	
    62	type RelaySocket = WebSocket<MaybeTlsStream<TcpStream>>;
    63	const RELAY_READ_TIMEOUT: Duration = Duration::from_millis(50);
    64	const RELAY_RECONNECT_DELAY: Duration = Duration::from_secs(3);
    65	
    66	pub(crate) fn spawn_relay_worker(
    67	    role: RelayRole,
    68	    generation: u64,
    69	    relay_tx: Sender<RelayEvent>,
    70	) -> Sender<RelayCommand> {
    71	    let (control_tx, control_rx) = mpsc::channel();
    72	    thread::spawn(move || run_relay_worker(role, generation, relay_tx, control_rx));
    73	    control_tx
    74	}
    75	
    76	fn run_relay_worker(
    77	    role: RelayRole,
    78	    generation: u64,
    79	    relay_tx: Sender<RelayEvent>,
    80	    control_rx: Receiver<RelayCommand>,
    81	) {
    82	    let mut pending = VecDeque::new();
    83	    loop {
    84	        match open_relay_socket(role) {
    85	            Ok(mut socket) => {
    86	                if relay_tx
    87	                    .send(RelayEvent::Connected { role, generation })
    88	                    .is_err()
    89	                {
    90	                    return;
    91	                }
    92	                match run_connected_relay(
    93	                    role,
    94	                    generation,
    95	                    &relay_tx,
    96	                    &control_rx,
    97	                    &mut pending,
    98	                    &mut socket,
    99	                ) {
   100	                    RelayWorkerResult::Reconnect => {}

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	//! In-memory `EventStore` backend.
     2	//!
     3	//! Used for tests and the pre-M15 web target. Every method is fully implemented
     4	//! against a `Mutex<MemState>` so tests cover the same logic that the LMDB
     5	//! backend will eventually call.
     6	//!
     7	//! See `docs/design/lmdb/trait.md` §5 ("Two backends in v1").
     8	//!
     9	//! Module layout:
    10	//!   mod.rs        — factory, `MemState`, `MemEventStore`, provenance helpers
    11	//!   store_impl.rs — `EventStore` trait impl (delegation to sub-modules)
    12	//!   insert.rs     — §7.1 insert invariants (replaceable, kind:5, normal)
    13	//!   query.rs      — read / scan methods
    14	//!   gc.rs         — claim / release / prune
    15	//!   domain.rs     — domain rows + migrations
    16	
    17	pub(super) mod domain;
    18	pub(super) mod gc;
    19	pub(super) mod insert;
    20	pub(super) mod query;
    21	mod store_impl;
    22	#[cfg(test)]
    23	mod tests;
    24	
    25	use std::collections::{BTreeSet, HashMap};
    26	use std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};
    27	
    28	use super::types::{
    29	    ClaimerId, ProvenanceEntry, RelayUrl, StoredEvent, TombstoneRow, WatermarkRow,
    30	};
    31	use super::StoreError;
    32	
    33	// ─── Constants ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    34	
    35	/// Default maximum pinned events per view (D8 / gc.md §2).
    36	pub(super) const DEFAULT_VIEW_CEILING: usize = 1_000;
    37	
    38	/// Hard global pinned ceiling (D8 / gc.md §2).
    39	pub(super) const MAX_PINNED_TOTAL: usize = 20_000;
    40	
    41	/// Maximum provenance entries kept per event.
    42	pub(super) const MAX_PROVENANCE_ENTRIES: usize = 32;
    43	
    44	/// Tombstones older than this many seconds are purged by `gc_step`.
    45	pub(super) const TOMBSTONE_MAX_AGE_SECS: u64 = 90 * 24 * 3600; // 90 days
    46	
    47	// ─── Shared storage type ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    48	
    49	/// Shared storage map for a single domain namespace.
    50	type DomainMap = Arc<Mutex<HashMap<Vec<u8>, Vec<u8>>>>;
    51	
    52	// ─── Inner state ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    53	
    54	pub(super) struct MemState {
    55	    /// Primary event store: hex id → StoredEvent.
    56	    pub(super) events: HashMap<String, StoredEvent>,
    57	
    58	    /// Tombstone rows: hex target_id → TombstoneRow.
    59	    pub(super) tombstones: HashMap<String, TombstoneRow>,
    60	
    61	    /// Address tombstones (kind:5 `a`-tag): "kind:pubkey:dtag" → TombstoneRow.
    62	    pub(super) addr_tombstones: HashMap<String, TombstoneRow>,
    63	
    64	    /// Provenance: hex event_id → sorted Vec<ProvenanceEntry>.
    65	    pub(super) provenance: HashMap<String, Vec<ProvenanceEntry>>,
    66	
    67	    /// Watermarks: (filter_hash_hex, relay_url) → WatermarkRow.
    68	    pub(super) watermarks: HashMap<(String, String), WatermarkRow>,
    69	
    70	    /// Domain data per namespace.
    71	    pub(super) domain_data: HashMap<&'static str, DomainMap>,
    72	
    73	    /// Domain schema versions.
    74	    pub(super) domain_versions: HashMap<&'static str, u32>,
    75	
    76	    /// Claim budgets: claimer → max pinned.
    77	    pub(super) claim_budgets: HashMap<ClaimerId, usize>,
    78	
    79	    /// Current claims: claimer → BTreeSet of hex event ids.
    80	    /// BTreeSet gives idempotency per T25 — re-claiming a known id is a no-op.

Reality on master HEAD (`a92ed2e`): kernel substrate + planner + LMDB store + subscription pool + NIP-42 auth gate + publish engine **all SHIP**; protocol crates `nmp-signers` / `nmp-nip29` / `nmp-nip42` / `nmp-nip77` **all SHIP**; `nmp-highlighter-core` is a stub (M11.5 step 0); `apps/podcast/{podcast-core,podcast-feeds,podcast-audio,podcast-llm,podcast-rag}` shipped as scaffolds; `ios/NmpStress` (1,375 LOC) is the live shell; `ios/NmpPodcast` + `ios/NmpHighlighter` are tiny skeletons. FFI today is raw C JSON-over-string (`crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`); UniFFI migration is M14 (PLANNED). M0–M8 + M10.5 + M11 + M11.5 are tracked DONE in `docs/perf/orchestration-log.md` HB31.

docs/design/podcast-app-rebuild.md:13:A **pixel-perfect rebuild** of the canonical Swift podcast app at `/Users/pablofernandez/src/podcast` (8,793 LOC across 47 Swift files, see [`inventory.md`](podcast/inventory.md)) running on NMP. Every Swift view file in `PodcastApp/Views/` is copied verbatim into `ios/NmpPodcast/Views/`; only the data source is rewired. All business logic — services, models, view models, the LLM/RAG/transcription pipeline, audio orchestration, downloads, RSS, recommendations — moves into Rust extension modules behind the kernel substrate (`DomainModule`, `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `CapabilityModule`, `IdentityModule`).
docs/design/podcast-app-rebuild.md:17:**Scope clarification (additive vs strict parity).** [`docs/plan/m11-podcast.md`](../plan/m11-podcast.md) lists `ImportOpml` as an action. The reference Swift app has no OPML import (`AddPodcastView` is a single-URL form, `LibraryView` lists subscribed podcasts only). M11 ships `ImportOpml` as **a Rust-only `ActionModule` with no UI entry point** — no button, no new view, no change to any copied Swift file. Adding a UI element for OPML import to any `Views/` file would violate the pixel-parity gate (see [`copy.md`](podcast/copy.md) §Invariants). The action is callable only via the kernel's external API (e.g., a CLI or post-M11 Settings entry); it is deferred to post-M11 for any UI surface. All other M11 features map 1:1 to an existing Swift surface.

docs/plan/m11-podcast.md:27:Swift `Services/` (AIService, AudioService, DownloadService, GuestEnrichmentService, ImageCache, InsightService, PodcastIndexService, PodcastService, ProcessingQueue, RAGService, RecommendationService, ServiceContainer, TranscriptionService, VectorDatabase) **all move to Rust** as ActionModules + ProjectionCaches + capability bridges; Swift loses its Services/ directory entirely.
docs/plan/m11-podcast.md:29:Swift `Models/` (AITypes, Chapter, Episode, Guest, Insight, Podcast, Settings, Transcript) **all move to Rust** as DomainRecords inside `podcast-core` and sibling crates.
docs/plan/m11-podcast.md:31:Swift `ViewModels/` **disappear** — they become Rust ViewModules whose output crosses FFI as typed ViewBatch deltas.
docs/plan/m11-podcast.md:35:**Step 0 — copy step (UI-fidelity invariant lock):**
docs/plan/m11-podcast.md:37:- Copy every file in `/Users/pablofernandez/src/podcast/PodcastApp/Views/` into `ios/NmpPodcast/NmpPodcast/Views/` verbatim. Commit immediately. No edits except the minimum needed to compile against placeholder data sources (`// MARK: NMP-WIRE` markers).
docs/plan/m11-podcast.md:38:- Copy `Resources/Assets.xcassets` and `Info.plist` (sanitized) verbatim.
docs/plan/m11-podcast.md:63:**Step 4 — wire each copied Swift view to its Rust view module:**
docs/plan/m11-podcast.md:66:- The Swift file shape stays the same; only the data source changes.
docs/plan/m11-podcast.md:78:- **UI parity:** side-by-side screenshot of every screen in `../podcast` vs `ios/NmpPodcast` matches at ≤ 1 px tolerance (font/rendering differences whitelisted explicitly in `docs/perf/m11/parity-screenshots.md`).
docs/plan/m11-podcast.md:79:- **Feature parity:** every user flow exercised in `/Users/pablofernandez/src/podcast/Tests/` (or its equivalent on the canonical Swift app) reproduced as a scripted Sonnet-agent run on `ios/NmpPodcast`. No "feature dropped" footnotes.
docs/plan/m11-podcast.md:87:- **Guest enrichment** populates guest cards via external lookup, identical to the Swift impl behavior.
docs/plan/m11-podcast.md:95:- Battery drain during 1 hour of background playback ≤ Swift baseline + 10 %.
docs/plan/m11-podcast.md:97:**Runnable artifact.** `ios/NmpPodcast` — distinct binary, same Rust kernel, different module set, **same UI as `../podcast`**. Report in `docs/perf/m11/podcast-app.md` documenting kernel-boundary verification, parity screenshots, and the perf measurements above.
docs/plan/m11.5-highlighter.md:10:A 1:1 rebuild of `/Users/pablofernandez/Work/hl/app` (Highlighter — Rust core + SwiftUI iOS app, 142 Swift files ≈ 39,200 LOC, Rust core 38 files ≈ 19,900 LOC) running on NMP. **UI is pixel-identical** to the reference iOS app; **all business logic** is in Rust extension modules driving the kernel via the substrate (`DomainModule`/`ViewModule`/`ActionModule`); **NIP-29 lives in a dedicated `nmp-nip29` protocol crate** consumed by the per-app generated crate.
docs/plan/m11.5-highlighter.md:18:- **`/Users/pablofernandez/Work/hl/app/ios/Highlighter/Sources/Highlighter/`** — the existing SwiftUI app (142 files; see `app-survey.md` §4 for feature-folder map). **Source of truth for UI and feature behavior. Every view in `Features/` is copied verbatim into `ios/NmpHighlighter/Sources/NmpHighlighter/Features/` as step 1; only the data source is rewired.**
docs/plan/m11.5-highlighter.md:25:| Swift `Features/` group | Files | NMP target |
docs/plan/m11.5-highlighter.md:42:Swift `Services/`-equivalent (the various reactive stores in `Communities/RoomStore.swift`, `ChatStore.swift`, `RoomExplorerStore.swift`, etc., plus `Session/SafeHighlighterCore.swift`) **all move to Rust** as ViewModules + ActionModules; the Swift store shells survive as thin Observable wrappers around NMP's generated `@JoinedGroups` / `@GroupChat` / etc. accessors.
docs/plan/m11.5-highlighter.md:44:Swift `Models/`-equivalent (`CommunitySummary`, `HighlightRecord`, `ChatMessageRecord`, `HydratedHighlight`, etc.) **all move to Rust** as `DomainRecord`s inside their owning protocol crates (group records in `nmp-nip29`, highlight records in `nmp-nip84`, etc.).

    44	#[no_mangle]
    45	pub extern "C" fn nmp_app_new() -> *mut NmpApp {
    46	    let (command_tx, command_rx) = mpsc::channel();
    47	    let (update_tx, update_rx) = mpsc::channel();
    48	    let update_callback: Arc<Mutex<Option<UpdateCallbackRegistration>>> =
    49	        Arc::new(Mutex::new(None));
    50	    let listener_callback = Arc::clone(&update_callback);
    51	    let actor = thread::spawn(move || run_actor(command_rx, update_tx));
    52	    let update_listener = thread::spawn(move || {
    53	        while let Ok(update) = update_rx.recv() {
    54	            let Ok(payload) = CString::new(update) else {
    55	                continue;
    56	            };
    57	            let callback = listener_callback.lock().ok().and_then(|guard| *guard);
    58	            if let Some(registration) = callback {
    59	                (registration.callback)(registration.context as *mut c_void, payload.as_ptr());
    60	            }
    61	        }
    62	    });
    63	
    64	    Box::into_raw(Box::new(NmpApp {
    65	        tx: command_tx,
    66	        update_callback,
    67	        actor: Mutex::new(Some(actor)),
    68	        update_listener: Mutex::new(Some(update_listener)),
    69	    }))
    70	}
    71	
    72	// SAFETY: `app` is a raw pointer from `nmp_app_new()`. The function is `extern "C"` (callable
    73	// from Swift/C) so it cannot be marked `unsafe` at the Rust level; the caller guarantees the
    74	// pointer contract. The `allow` suppresses the clippy::not_unsafe_ptr_arg_deref lint which
    75	// does not distinguish between `extern "C"` FFI boundaries and ordinary Rust functions.
    76	#[allow(clippy::not_unsafe_ptr_arg_deref)]
    77	#[no_mangle]
    78	pub extern "C" fn nmp_app_free(app: *mut NmpApp) {
    79	    if !app.is_null() {
    80	        // SAFETY: caller guarantees app is a valid pointer allocated by nmp_app_new().
    81	        unsafe {
    82	            drop(Box::from_raw(app));
    83	        }
    84	    }
    85	}
    86	
    87	#[no_mangle]
    88	pub extern "C" fn nmp_app_set_update_callback(
    89	    app: *mut NmpApp,
    90	    context: *mut c_void,
    91	    callback: Option<UpdateCallback>,
    92	) {
    93	    let Some(app) = app_ref(app) else {
    94	        return;
    95	    };
    96	    let Ok(mut slot) = app.update_callback.lock() else {
    97	        return;
    98	    };
    99	    *slot = callback.map(|callback| UpdateCallbackRegistration {
   100	        context: context as usize,
   101	        callback,
   102	    });
   103	}
   104	
   105	#[no_mangle]
   106	pub extern "C" fn nmp_app_start(
   107	    app: *mut NmpApp,
   108	    _events_per_second: c_uint,
   109	    visible_limit: c_uint,
   110	    emit_hz: c_uint,
   111	) {
   112	    let Some(app) = app_ref(app) else {
   113	        return;
   114	    };
   115	
   116	    let _ = app.tx.send(ActorCommand::Start {
   117	        visible_limit: clamp_visible(visible_limit),
   118	        emit_hz: clamp_emit_hz(emit_hz),
   119	    });
   120	}

     1	//! Publishing pipeline (M7 substrate, scaffolded ahead of M3 / M6 / M8).
     2	//!
     3	//! This module owns the substrate-level publish engine: the action shape, the
     4	//! per-(event, relay) state machine, the durable retry queue contract, and the
     5	//! `PublishStatusView` payload. The kernel actor and relay-manager wiring land
     6	//! when their respective milestones ship (#43 Signer, #46 RelayManager, M3
     7	//! LMDB persistence). Until then the engine consumes minimal trait shims that
     8	//! the downstream milestones will satisfy without forcing the publish
     9	//! pipeline to be rewritten — see `traits.rs`.
    10	//!
    11	//! Doctrine map:
    12	//! - D3 (outbox automatic): `PublishTarget::Auto` resolves via `OutboxResolver`
    13	//!   — the engine never picks relays from a hardcoded constant.
    14	//! - D4 (single writer per fact): per-(event, relay) status is owned by the
    15	//!   engine; the snapshot is derived from it.
    16	//! - D5 (snapshots bounded by what's open): the view payload is small and
    17	//!   only carries currently-pending plus a bounded recent window.
    18	//! - D6 (errors never cross FFI as exceptions): per-relay publish failures
    19	//!   surface as `RecentFailure` entries on the snapshot plus a coarse
    20	//!   `PublishOutcome::Mixed` / `FailedAfterRetries` on the action ledger.
    21	//!   Engine-level `PublishEngineError` values (`DuplicateHandle`, `NoTargets`,
    22	//!   `Store`) are mapped by `engine::engine_error_to_failure` into the same
    23	//!   `RecentFailure` shape so the FFI boundary only ever sees state, never
    24	//!   an exception or `Result<T, E>`.
    25	//! - D7 (capabilities report): the `RelayDispatcher` shim returns purely
    26	//!   descriptive `RelayAck { ok, code, message, details }` values; the
    27	//!   engine's `classify_ack` (in `state.rs`) is the only place that maps
    28	//!   ack codes to retry policy.
    29	//! - D8 (≤60 Hz/view): the view payload exposes a monotonic `rev` so the
    30	//!   projection bridge can coalesce publish-status changes under the view
    31	//!   emission budget.
    32	
    33	mod action;
    34	mod engine;
    35	mod state;
    36	#[cfg(test)]
    37	mod tests;
    38	mod traits;
    39	mod view;
    40	
    41	pub use action::{
    42	    PublishAction, PublishHandle, PublishModule, PublishOutcome, PublishStep, PublishTarget,
    43	    RelayUrl,
    44	};
    45	pub use engine::{
    46	    engine_error_to_failure, outcome_of, PublishEngine, PublishEngineError,
    47	    ENGINE_FAILURE_RELAY_URL,
    48	};
    49	pub use state::{PerRelayState, PublishAttempt, RelayAck, RelayPlan, RetryPolicy, RetryVerdict};
    50	pub use traits::{
    51	    InMemoryPublishStore, NoopOutboxResolver, NoopSigner, OutboxResolver, PublishRecord,
    52	    PublishStore, PublishStoreError, RelayDispatcher, ReplayDispatcher, Signer, SignerError,
    53	    StaticOutbox,
    54	};
    55	pub use view::{
    56	    EventPublishStatus, PublishStatusSnapshot, PublishStatusSpec, PublishStatusView, RecentFailure,
    57	    RecentSuccess,
    58	};

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	//! M4 — NIP-77 negentropy sync engine.
     2	//!
     3	//! Implements the cardinal doctrine **D2 — negentropy first, REQ second** by
     4	//! providing the sync substrate that NMP's planner consults before issuing
     5	//! REQ frames.  Transport-agnostic: the reconciler exchanges opaque byte
     6	//! payloads; the relay protocol envelope (`NEG-OPEN`/`NEG-MSG`/`NEG-CLOSE`)
     7	//! and the WebSocket wire are layered on top via [`wire`].
     8	//!
     9	//! ## Module map
    10	//!
    11	//! | Module | Responsibility |
    12	//! |---|---|
    13	//! | [`reconciler`] | Wraps `negentropy::Negentropy` with a deterministic step API. |
    14	//! | [`wire`] | Encodes / decodes NIP-77 client + relay frames as JSON arrays. |
    15	//! | [`capability`] | Per-relay `supports_nip77` cache with a probe state machine. |
    16	//! | [`capability_domain`] | `DomainModule` for persistent capability rows. |
    17	//! | [`coverage_gate`] | Maps `Coverage` → `SyncStrategy` (skip REQ / REQ-since / sync-then-REQ). |
    18	//! | [`planner_gate`] | Applies the coverage strategy in-place to a `CompiledPlan`. |
    19	//! | [`triggers`] | Foreground / view-open-gap / relay-reconnect trigger fan-out. |
    20	//! | [`metrics`] | Per-(filter, relay) `bytes_on_wire_via_neg` + `bytes_saved_vs_req` counters. |
    21	//! | [`run_sync`] | `RunSync` `ActionModule` for manual reconciliation. |
    22	//!
    23	//! ## Doctrine
    24	//!
    25	//! * **D2** — negentropy first.  [`coverage_gate::decide_strategy`] returns
    26	//!   [`SyncStrategy::SkipReq`] when the watermark is `CompleteAsOf` and fresh,
    27	//!   [`SyncStrategy::NegThenReq`] when the relay supports NIP-77 and the gap is
    28	//!   large, and [`SyncStrategy::ReqSince`] only as the explicit fallback path.
    29	//! * **D6** — errors never cross FFI.  All public errors here are internal
    30	//!   `Result` returns; mapping to `toast`/`busy` state happens at the actor /
    31	//!   `RunSync` action boundary, not in the engine.
    32	//! * **D8** — reconciliation is working-set-bounded.  The reconciler's frame
    33	//!   budget is capped at `64 KiB` per step and the trigger engine deduplicates
    34	//!   redundant work-items per `(filter_hash, relay_url)` before dispatching.
    35	
    36	pub mod capability;
    37	pub mod capability_domain;
    38	pub mod coverage_gate;
    39	pub mod metrics;
    40	pub mod planner_gate;
    41	pub mod reconciler;
    42	pub mod run_sync;
    43	pub mod triggers;
    44	pub mod wire;
    45	
    46	#[cfg(test)]
    47	#[path = "reconciler_tests.rs"]
    48	mod reconciler_tests;
    49	
    50	#[cfg(test)]
    51	#[path = "planner_gate_tests.rs"]
    52	mod planner_gate_tests;
    53	
    54	pub use capability::{
    55	    CapabilityCache, CapabilityProbe, InMemoryCapabilityCache, ProbeOutcome, ProbeState,
    56	    RelayCapabilities,
    57	};
    58	pub use capability_domain::{CapabilityDomain, CapabilityRow};
    59	pub use coverage_gate::{decide_strategy, GateInputs, SyncStrategy, COVERAGE_THRESHOLD_PCT};
    60	pub use metrics::{MetricsSnapshot, RelayFilterKey, SyncMetrics};
    61	pub use planner_gate::{apply_coverage_filter, CoverageReport, GateDecision};
    62	pub use reconciler::{
    63	    Reconciler, ReconcilerError, ReconcilerOutcome, ReconcilerRole, SyncedItem,
    64	};
    65	pub use run_sync::{
    66	    RunSync, RunSyncAction, RunSyncOutput, RunSyncStep, ACTION_NAMESPACE as RUN_SYNC_NAMESPACE,
    67	};
    68	pub use triggers::{ReconcileWork, TriggerEngine, TriggerEvent};
    69	pub use wire::{ClientFrame, RelayFrame, WireError};
    70	
    71	/// Frame-size cap shared by the reconciler + wire encoder.  64 KiB matches
    72	/// the budget used by real NIP-77 relay implementations (strfry,
    73	/// `relay-builder`) and keeps step latency bounded on mobile sockets — a
    74	/// 64 KiB payload fits in roughly 8–10 WebSocket TCP segments on LTE/5G.
    75	/// Smaller values inflate round-trip count without reducing bytes-on-wire
    76	/// (most of which are 32-byte id payloads, not protocol overhead).  D8 —
    77	/// working-set bounded.
    78	pub const FRAME_SIZE_LIMIT: u64 = 64 * 1024;
