     1	//! Kind:10002 (NIP-65 relay list) ingest.
     2	
     3	use super::super::*;
     4	
     5	impl Kernel {
     6	    /// Ingest a kind:10002 NIP-65 relay-list event into `author_relay_lists`.
     7	    ///
     8	    /// Only called after `verify_and_persist` returns `Inserted | Replaced` (D4).
     9	    ///
    10	    /// ## Empty-list semantics
    11	    ///
    12	    /// If the canonical kind:10002 carries an **empty** relay list (all three
    13	    /// buckets are empty), this means the author has explicitly cleared their
    14	    /// NIP-65 metadata.  In that case the existing cache entry is *removed*
    15	    /// rather than left stale — an empty-but-canonical event must not allow the
    16	    /// old relay list to persist indefinitely.
    17	    ///
    18	    /// ## Supersession guard
    19	    ///
    20	    /// The local cache guard uses strict `>` on `created_at` with a
    21	    /// lexicographic event-id tiebreak, mirroring the store's supersession
    22	    /// logic exactly.  This function is only reached after the store confirmed
    23	    /// this event won, so the guard is belt-and-suspenders protection against
    24	    /// any race or re-ordering at the call site.
    25	    pub(in crate::kernel) fn ingest_relay_list(&mut self, event: NostrEvent) {
    26	        let relay_list = parse_relay_list(&event.id, event.created_at, &event.tags);
    27	
    28	        // Empty relay list from a canonical newer event: author cleared NIP-65.
    29	        // Remove the stale cache entry so it does not outlive the author's intent.
    30	        if relay_list.read_relays.is_empty()
    31	            && relay_list.write_relays.is_empty()
    32	            && relay_list.both_relays.is_empty()
    33	        {
    34	            self.author_relay_lists.remove(&event.pubkey);
    35	            return;
    36	        }
    37	
    38	        // This function is only called after verify_and_persist returned
    39	        // Inserted | Replaced, so the store already enforced strict `>` with
    40	        // lexicographic event-id tiebreak. The local cache guard below is a
    41	        // belt-and-suspenders check that mirrors the store's supersession
    42	        // logic exactly (strict `>` on timestamp; same-ts resolved by
    43	        // lexicographically smaller event id wins).
    44	        let should_replace = self
    45	            .author_relay_lists
    46	            .get(&event.pubkey)
    47	            .map(|current| {
    48	                relay_list.created_at > current.created_at
    49	                    || (relay_list.created_at == current.created_at
    50	                        && event.id < current.event_id)
    51	            })
    52	            .unwrap_or(true);
    53	        if should_replace {
    54	            self.log(format!(
    55	                "NIP-65 {} read={} write={} both={}",
    56	                short_hex(&event.pubkey),
    57	                relay_list.read_relays.len(),
    58	                relay_list.write_relays.len(),
    59	                relay_list.both_relays.len()
    60	            ));
    61	            self.author_relay_lists.insert(event.pubkey, relay_list);
    62	        }
    63	    }
    64	}
     1	//! Kernel request coordination — relay state transitions, startup REQs, view
     2	//! open/close dispatch, and the core `req` / `defer_outbound` / `record_tx`
     3	//! primitives.
     4	//!
     5	//! Per-view request builders are in sibling files:
     6	//! - `profile.rs` — profile/author open/close/claim/release
     7	//! - `thread.rs`  — thread open/close/hydration
     8	
     9	mod profile;
    10	mod thread;
    11	
    12	use super::*;
    13	
    14	impl Kernel {
    15	    pub(crate) fn relay_connecting(&mut self, role: RelayRole) {
    16	        let relay = self.relay_mut(role);
    17	        relay.connection = "connecting".to_string();
    18	        self.changed_since_emit = true;
    19	        self.log(format!("connecting {} relay {}", role.key(), role.url()));
    20	    }
    21	
    22	    pub(crate) fn relay_connected(&mut self, role: RelayRole) {
    23	        let relay = self.relay_mut(role);
    24	        relay.connection = "connected".to_string();
    25	        relay.connected_at = Some(Instant::now());
    26	        relay.last_error = None;
    27	        self.changed_since_emit = true;
    28	        self.log(format!("{} relay connected", role.key()));
    29	    }
    30	
    31	    pub(crate) fn relay_failed(&mut self, role: RelayRole, error: String) {
    32	        let relay = self.relay_mut(role);
    33	        relay.connection = "backing_off".to_string();
    34	        relay.last_error = Some(truncate(&error, 160));
    35	        relay.reconnect_count = relay.reconnect_count.saturating_add(1);
    36	        self.thread_ids_inflight = false;
    37	        self.thread_replies_inflight = false;
    38	        self.changed_since_emit = true;
    39	        self.log(format!(
    40	            "{} relay error: {}",
    41	            role.key(),
    42	            truncate(&error, 140)
    43	        ));
    44	        for sub in self.wire_subs.values_mut() {
    45	            if sub.role == role && sub.state != "closed" {
    46	                sub.state = "retrying".to_string();
    47	            }
    48	        }
    49	    }
    50	
    51	    pub(crate) fn relay_closed(&mut self, role: RelayRole) {
    52	        self.relay_mut(role).connection = "closed".to_string();
    53	        for sub in self.wire_subs.values_mut() {
    54	            if sub.role == role {
    55	                sub.state = "closed".to_string();
    56	            }
    57	        }
    58	        self.changed_since_emit = true;
    59	    }
    60	
    61	    pub(crate) fn startup_requests(&mut self) -> Vec<OutboundMessage> {
    62	        self.contacts_deadline = Some(Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(3));
    63	        let seeds = seed_accounts();
    64	        let seed_pubkeys = seeds.iter().map(|seed| seed.pubkey).collect::<Vec<_>>();
    65	
    66	        for seed in &seeds {
    67	            self.timeline_authors.insert(seed.pubkey.to_string());
    68	            self.log(format!(
    69	                "seed account: {} {}",
    70	                seed.name,
    71	                short_hex(seed.pubkey)
    72	            ));
    73	        }
    74	
    75	        let mut requests = Vec::new();
    76	        requests.push(self.req(
    77	            RelayRole::Content,
    78	            "seed-bootstrap",
    79	            "seed author bootstrap timeline",
    80	            json!({"kinds":[1,6],"authors":seed_pubkeys.clone(),"limit":80}),

 6. End every section with `See also:` listing `xref` exactly as `[NN — title](NN-name.md)`.

 1. Read this PLAN + your section's brief + cited prereqs (briefs or drafted sections) before drafting.
 2. Honor the `status` field strictly. SHIPS may cite `crates/`/`apps/`/`ios/` `path:line`; LANDED cites design + ADRs + partial code; PLANNED cites plan files + ADRs only.
-3. Verify every `path:line` you cite by Reading the file in the same commit (`a92ed2e` or later on master).
+3. Verify every `path:line` you cite by reading the file at the current master tip.
 4. Keep your file ≤ 300 LOC. If exceeded, split per the brief's hint (e.g. 05a/05b) and update the TOC in a follow-up.
 5. Include ≥3 anti-patterns and ≥2 concrete deliverables from the brief.
 6. End every section with `See also:` listing `xref` exactly as `[NN — title](NN-name.md)`.

 5. Include ≥3 anti-patterns and ≥2 concrete deliverables from the brief.
 6. End every section with `See also:` listing `xref` exactly as `[NN — title](NN-name.md)`.

 1. Read this PLAN + your section's brief + cited prereqs (briefs or drafted sections) before drafting.
 2. Honor the `status` field strictly. SHIPS may cite `crates/`/`apps/`/`ios/` `path:line`; LANDED cites design + ADRs + partial code; PLANNED cites plan files + ADRs only.
-3. Verify every `path:line` you cite by Reading the file in the same commit (`a92ed2e` or later on master).
+3. Verify every `path:line` you cite by reading the file at the current master tip.
 4. Keep your file ≤ 300 LOC. If exceeded, split per the brief's hint (e.g. 05a/05b) and update the TOC in a follow-up.
 5. Include ≥3 anti-patterns and ≥2 concrete deliverables from the brief.
 6. End every section with `See also:` listing `xref` exactly as `[NN — title](NN-name.md)`.

 1. Read this PLAN + your section's brief + cited prereqs (briefs or drafted sections) before drafting.
 2. Honor the `status` field strictly. SHIPS may cite `crates/`/`apps/`/`ios/` `path:line`; LANDED cites design + ADRs + partial code; PLANNED cites plan files + ADRs only.
-3. Verify every `path:line` you cite by Reading the file in the same commit (`a92ed2e` or later on master).
+3. Verify every `path:line` you cite by reading the file at the current master tip.
 4. Keep your file ≤ 300 LOC. If exceeded, split per the brief's hint (e.g. 05a/05b) and update the TOC in a follow-up.
 5. Include ≥3 anti-patterns and ≥2 concrete deliverables from the brief.
 6. End every section with `See also:` listing `xref` exactly as `[NN — title](NN-name.md)`.

 1. Read this PLAN + your section's brief + cited prereqs (briefs or drafted sections) before drafting.
 2. Honor the `status` field strictly. SHIPS may cite `crates/`/`apps/`/`ios/` `path:line`; LANDED cites design + ADRs + partial code; PLANNED cites plan files + ADRs only.
-3. Verify every `path:line` you cite by Reading the file in the same commit (`a92ed2e` or later on master).
+3. Verify every `path:line` you cite by reading the file at the current master tip.
 4. Keep your file ≤ 300 LOC. If exceeded, split per the brief's hint (e.g. 05a/05b) and update the TOC in a follow-up.
 5. Include ≥3 anti-patterns and ≥2 concrete deliverables from the brief.
 6. End every section with `See also:` listing `xref` exactly as `[NN — title](NN-name.md)`.

 1. Read this PLAN + your section's brief + cited prereqs (briefs or drafted sections) before drafting.
 2. Honor the `status` field strictly. SHIPS may cite `crates/`/`apps/`/`ios/` `path:line`; LANDED cites design + ADRs + partial code; PLANNED cites plan files + ADRs only.

+3. Verify every `path:line` you cite by reading the file at the current master tip.
 4. Keep your file ≤ 300 LOC. If exceeded, split per the brief's hint (e.g. 05a/05b) and update the TOC in a follow-up.
 5. Include ≥3 anti-patterns and ≥2 concrete deliverables from the brief.
 6. End every section with `See also:` listing `xref` exactly as `[NN — title](NN-name.md)`.

 1. Read this PLAN + your section's brief + cited prereqs (briefs or drafted sections) before drafting.
 2. Honor the `status` field strictly. SHIPS may cite `crates/`/`apps/`/`ios/` `path:line`; LANDED cites design + ADRs + partial code; PLANNED cites plan files + ADRs only.
-3. Verify every `path:line` you cite by Reading the file in the same commit (`a92ed2e` or later on master).
+3. Verify every `path:line` you cite by reading the file at the current master tip.
 4. Keep your file ≤ 300 LOC. If exceeded, split per the brief's hint (e.g. 05a/05b) and update the TOC in a follow-up.
 5. Include ≥3 anti-patterns and ≥2 concrete deliverables from the brief.
 6. End every section with `See also:` listing `xref` exactly as `[NN — title](NN-name.md)`.

 1. Read this PLAN + your section's brief + cited prereqs (briefs or drafted sections) before drafting.
 2. Honor the `status` field strictly. SHIPS may cite `crates/`/`apps/`/`ios/` `path:line`; LANDED cites design + ADRs + partial code; PLANNED cites plan files + ADRs only.
-3. Verify every `path:line` you cite by Reading the file in the same commit (`a92ed2e` or later on master).
+3. Verify every `path:line` you cite by reading the file at the current master tip.
 4. Keep your file ≤ 300 LOC. If exceeded, split per the brief's hint (e.g. 05a/05b) and update the TOC in a follow-up.
 5. Include ≥3 anti-patterns and ≥2 concrete deliverables from the brief.
 6. End every section with `See also:` listing `xref` exactly as `[NN — title](NN-name.md)`.

 1. Read this PLAN + your section's brief + cited prereqs (briefs or drafted sections) before drafting.
 2. Honor the `status` field strictly. SHIPS may cite `crates/`/`apps/`/`ios/` `path:line`; LANDED cites design + ADRs + partial code; PLANNED cites plan files + ADRs only.
-3. Verify every `path:line` you cite by Reading the file in the same commit (`a92ed2e` or later on master).
+3. Verify every `path:line` you cite by reading the file at the current master tip.
 4. Keep your file ≤ 300 LOC. If exceeded, split per the brief's hint (e.g. 05a/05b) and update the TOC in a follow-up.
 5. Include ≥3 anti-patterns and ≥2 concrete deliverables from the brief.
 6. End every section with `See also:` listing `xref` exactly as `[NN — title](NN-name.md)`.

 5. Include ≥3 anti-patterns and ≥2 concrete deliverables from the brief.
 6. End every section with `See also:` listing `xref` exactly as `[NN — title](NN-name.md)`.

 1. Read this PLAN + your section's brief + cited prereqs (briefs or drafted sections) before drafting.
 2. Honor the `status` field strictly. SHIPS may cite `crates/`/`apps/`/`ios/` `path:line`; LANDED cites design + ADRs + partial code; PLANNED cites plan files + ADRs only.
-3. Verify every `path:line` you cite by Reading the file in the same commit (`a92ed2e` or later on master).
+3. Verify every `path:line` you cite by reading the file at the current master tip.
 4. Keep your file ≤ 300 LOC. If exceeded, split per the brief's hint (e.g. 05a/05b) and update the TOC in a follow-up.
 5. Include ≥3 anti-patterns and ≥2 concrete deliverables from the brief.
 6. End every section with `See also:` listing `xref` exactly as `[NN — title](NN-name.md)`.

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "find . -path './.git' -prune -o -type f \\( -name '*.rs' -o -name '*.md' -o -name '*.swift' -o -name '*.toml' -o -name '*.yml' -o -name '*.yaml' \\) -print | while IFS= read -r f; do case \""'$f" in *Cargo.lock|*target/*|*DerivedData*|*/Assets.xcassets/*|*/Core/Generated/*|*/docs/perf/codex-reviews/*) continue;; esac; lines=$(wc -l < "$f"); if [ "$lines" -gt 500 ]; then printf '"'%5d %s\\n' \""'$lines" "$f"; fi; done | sort -nr' in /Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/.claude/worktrees/agent-a1a6c8d868b9ac3f0

 1. Read this PLAN + your section's brief + cited prereqs (briefs or drafted sections) before drafting.
 2. Honor the `status` field strictly. SHIPS may cite `crates/`/`apps/`/`ios/` `path:line`; LANDED cites design + ADRs + partial code; PLANNED cites plan files + ADRs only.

 1. Read this PLAN + your section's brief + cited prereqs (briefs or drafted sections) before drafting.
 2. Honor the `status` field strictly. SHIPS may cite `crates/`/`apps/`/`ios/` `path:line`; LANDED cites design + ADRs + partial code; PLANNED cites plan files + ADRs only.
-3. Verify every `path:line` you cite by Reading the file in the same commit (`a92ed2e` or later on master).
+3. Verify every `path:line` you cite by reading the file at the current master tip.
 4. Keep your file ≤ 300 LOC. If exceeded, split per the brief's hint (e.g. 05a/05b) and update the TOC in a follow-up.
 5. Include ≥3 anti-patterns and ≥2 concrete deliverables from the brief.
 6. End every section with `See also:` listing `xref` exactly as `[NN — title](NN-name.md)`.

-The wire-emitter (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/wire.rs`, to be added) diffs the new plan against the current wire-sub registry: opens new REQs, closes orphaned ones, leaves stable assignments untouched.
+The wire-emitter (`crates/nmp-core/src/subs/wire.rs`) diffs the new plan against the current wire-sub registry: opens new REQs, closes orphaned ones, leaves stable assignments untouched.
 
 ## 3.1 Stage 1 — Resolve authors to mailboxes
 
-Inputs: every `LogicalInterest` with non-empty `shape.authors` or non-empty `shape.tags[#p]`; the mailbox cache populated by `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233`).
+Inputs: every `LogicalInterest` with non-empty `shape.authors` or non-empty `shape.tags[#p]`; the mailbox cache populated by `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest/relay_list.rs:25-63`).
 
 Output: an `AuthorRouting` per author per direction:
 
@@ -110,6 +110,7 @@
 6. `A.lifecycle == B.lifecycle`. Tailing and one-shot do not merge (one-shot would never close).
 7. `A.event_ids` and `B.event_ids`: merge by union, capped at the relay's per-filter `ids` limit.
 8. **Rule 8 (address-pointer union).** `A.addresses` and `B.addresses` merge by `A.addresses ∪ B.addresses`, provided their other constraints (`authors`, `kinds`, `tags`, time, lifecycle) merge per Rules 1–7. Overlapping coordinates with differing time or lifecycle constraints do **not** merge — the compiler emits per-address sub-shapes so each `NaddrCoord`'s routing is independent. The union cap is the relay's per-filter `#a` value limit (default 1000). Address routing uses `NaddrCoord::pubkey` as the `AuthorRouting` input (Outbox direction, Stage 1), so the relays chosen are the addressed author's write relays — the same path as an `authors`-bearing filter for that pubkey.
+9. **Rule 9 (`relay_pin` equality).** `A.relay_pin == B.relay_pin`; `None` does not absorb `Some(_)`. Host-pinned interests route through Case E before Cases A-D, and identical pins may still coalesce via Rules 1-8.
 
 When mergeable, the merged shape is `{ authors: A.authors ∪ B.authors, addresses: A.addresses ∪ B.addresses, ... }`. The merged interest tracks both originating `InterestId`s so per-event dispatch back to consumers stays correct.
 
@@ -168,7 +169,7 @@
 
 Properties:
 
-- **Recompilation with no change ⇒ same plan-id.** If `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:218-221`) deduplicates and decides not to replace a stale mailbox, no plan-id churn.
+- **Recompilation with no change ⇒ same plan-id.** If `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest/relay_list.rs:38-61`) deduplicates and decides not to replace a stale mailbox, no plan-id churn.
 - **Adding an interest changes plan-id even if no new wire REQ results.** Two interests can merge into the same SubShape; the plan-id changes because the *interest set* changed. The platform diagnostic correctly reports "logical-interest count went up; wire-sub count did not."
 - **A new kind:10002 for a referenced author changes plan-id.** The hash only covers mailboxes for authors the current interest set touches, so a kind:10002 for an unrelated pubkey (not in any active interest's `authors` or `#p`) does not churn plan-ids.
 - **A new kind:10002 for an unreferenced author does NOT change plan-id.** This is the key
@@ -183,7 +184,7 @@
 
 ## 3.5 Migration of existing functions
 
-This is the binding contract: each function in `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs` and `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs` either disappears, becomes thin glue over the compiler, or graduates into a typed module. The compiler does not coexist with the old planner; M2 replaces it.
+This is the binding contract: each function in `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests/` and `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest/` either disappears, becomes thin glue over the compiler, or graduates into a typed module. The compiler does not coexist with the old planner; M2 replaces it.
 
 | Current function (file:line) | M2 replacement |
 |---|---|

 ## 3.1 Stage 1 — Resolve authors to mailboxes
 
-Inputs: every `LogicalInterest` with non-empty `shape.authors` or non-empty `shape.tags[#p]`; the mailbox cache populated by `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233`).
+Inputs: every `LogicalInterest` with non-empty `shape.authors` or non-empty `shape.tags[#p]`; the mailbox cache populated by `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest/relay_list.rs:25-63`).
 
 Output: an `AuthorRouting` per author per direction:
 
@@ -110,6 +110,7 @@
 6. `A.lifecycle == B.lifecycle`. Tailing and one-shot do not merge (one-shot would never close).
 7. `A.event_ids` and `B.event_ids`: merge by union, capped at the relay's per-filter `ids` limit.
 8. **Rule 8 (address-pointer union).** `A.addresses` and `B.addresses` merge by `A.addresses ∪ B.addresses`, provided their other constraints (`authors`, `kinds`, `tags`, time, lifecycle) merge per Rules 1–7. Overlapping coordinates with differing time or lifecycle constraints do **not** merge — the compiler emits per-address sub-shapes so each `NaddrCoord`'s routing is independent. The union cap is the relay's per-filter `#a` value limit (default 1000). Address routing uses `NaddrCoord::pubkey` as the `AuthorRouting` input (Outbox direction, Stage 1), so the relays chosen are the addressed author's write relays — the same path as an `authors`-bearing filter for that pubkey.
+9. **Rule 9 (`relay_pin` equality).** `A.relay_pin == B.relay_pin`; `None` does not absorb `Some(_)`. Host-pinned interests route through Case E before Cases A-D, and identical pins may still coalesce via Rules 1-8.
 
 When mergeable, the merged shape is `{ authors: A.authors ∪ B.authors, addresses: A.addresses ∪ B.addresses, ... }`. The merged interest tracks both originating `InterestId`s so per-event dispatch back to consumers stays correct.
 
@@ -168,7 +169,7 @@
 
 Properties:
 
-- **Recompilation with no change ⇒ same plan-id.** If `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:218-221`) deduplicates and decides not to replace a stale mailbox, no plan-id churn.
+- **Recompilation with no change ⇒ same plan-id.** If `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest/relay_list.rs:38-61`) deduplicates and decides not to replace a stale mailbox, no plan-id churn.
 - **Adding an interest changes plan-id even if no new wire REQ results.** Two interests can merge into the same SubShape; the plan-id changes because the *interest set* changed. The platform diagnostic correctly reports "logical-interest count went up; wire-sub count did not."
 - **A new kind:10002 for a referenced author changes plan-id.** The hash only covers mailboxes for authors the current interest set touches, so a kind:10002 for an unrelated pubkey (not in any active interest's `authors` or `#p`) does not churn plan-ids.
 - **A new kind:10002 for an unreferenced author does NOT change plan-id.** This is the key
@@ -183,7 +184,7 @@
 
 ## 3.5 Migration of existing functions
 
-This is the binding contract: each function in `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs` and `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs` either disappears, becomes thin glue over the compiler, or graduates into a typed module. The compiler does not coexist with the old planner; M2 replaces it.
+This is the binding contract: each function in `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests/` and `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest/` either disappears, becomes thin glue over the compiler, or graduates into a typed module. The compiler does not coexist with the old planner; M2 replaces it.
 
 | Current function (file:line) | M2 replacement |
 |---|---|

docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:191:| `startup_requests` (requests.rs:50-106) | **Relocated outside `nmp-core` per D0.** The seed timeline / kind:0 / kind:10002 / kind:3 bootstrap is social graph knowledge — not kernel substrate. It moves to `nmp-nip01` / `nmp-nip02` / the demo app's startup sequence. `nmp-core` only executes interests registered by modules; it does not hard-code which interests those are. The TEST_PUBKEY-specific bootstrap (line 71-82) moves to the demo app (or a test fixture in `nmp-testing`). The kernel's `ActorStart` handler calls `compiler.recompile(Trigger::Startup)`, which is a no-op until a module registers interests. |
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:192:| `open_author` (requests.rs:118-140) | Registers three `LogicalInterest`s scoped `ActiveAccount` (kind:10002, kind:0, kinds:1+6 for author); calls `compiler.recompile(Trigger::ViewOpen)`. Refcount stays — but it lives on `InterestId` now, not on `ViewInterest { key, refcount }`. The `can_send` gate disappears: the compiler always produces a plan; the wire-emitter is the only thing that may queue deferrals. |
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:193:| `open_thread` (requests.rs:142-168) | Registers a `Thread { event_id }` view-module spec; the view module returns interests with `event_ids` and `#e`-tag shapes. Hydration cascade in `prepare_thread_requests` (requests.rs:441-466) becomes part of the view module's `reduce` returning new interests when new event ids surface. |
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:194:| `open_firehose_tag` (requests.rs:170-200) | Registers one `LogicalInterest { shape: { kinds: [1], tags: { #t: [tag] } }, scope: ActiveAccount, lifecycle: Tailing }`. Routes to active-account read relays per §3.1 table. |
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:195:| `claim_profile` / `release_profile` (requests.rs:202-263) | Registers/unregisters one `LogicalInterest { shape: { authors: [pk], kinds: [0], limit: 1 }, lifecycle: OneShot }` per claim. Refcount of distinct consumers becomes the `InterestId` claim set inside the registry. **Dedup of (pk, kinds=[0]) across N timeline rows yields one merged SubShape and one wire REQ** — this is what bug-extinction "1000 avatars do not produce 1000 REQs" verifies. The refcount must be an integer counter, not a boolean presence flag — Applesauce gotcha `75ef7d5f` (`docs/research/applesauce/gotchas.md` §G2) shows the boolean model causes leaks when two consumers share a claim and one unsubscribes. |
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:196:| `close_author` / `close_thread` (requests.rs:265-311) | Drop interests by `InterestId`; recompile with `Trigger::ViewClose`. Wire-emitter closes orphaned REQs. The "warm-close" grace from the view-warmth doctrine (`docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3 "lifecycle") is the compiler's, not the view's — interests stay registered for the warmth window after their last claim. |
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:197:| `close_subscriptions_with_prefixes` (requests.rs:313-331) | **Deleted.** The wire-emitter closes by `WireSubId`, which is the compiler's diff output. String-prefix matching of sub-ids is a 2026-05-period scaffold that the compiler removes. |
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:198:| `pending_view_requests` (requests.rs:333-355) | Becomes `compiler.flush_deferred_for_relay(role, url)`: called when a relay reconnects (§4 trigger A3). The compiler resubmits its current plan against that relay's slot. |
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:199:| `firehose_requests` (requests.rs:357-372) | Replaced as described above for `open_firehose_tag`. The `diag-firehose-N` sub-id scheme goes away — `canonical_filter_hash` plus `plan_id` give stable identity. |
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:200:| `pending_profile_claim_requests` (requests.rs:374-388) | Disappears. Claims are interests; the compiler is the only thing that decides "this interest needs a REQ." |
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:201:| `profile_claim_request` (requests.rs:390-402) | Disappears. The compiler routes claimed-profile interests through Stage 1; indexer fallback (Stage 2) handles the no-mailbox case. |
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:202:| `author_requests` (requests.rs:404-439) | Disappears (replaced by `open_author`'s interest registration). |
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:203:| `prepare_thread_requests` / `enqueue_thread_*` / `maybe_open_thread_hydration` (requests.rs:441-528) | Move to a `ThreadViewModule` in `nmp-nip10`. The hydration cascade is `view_module.reduce(...)` returning additional interests as new event ids surface in store. |
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:204:| `req` (requests.rs:530-556) | **Deleted.** Replaced by the wire-emitter's `emit_req(relay_url, sub_id, filter)`. No call site outside the wire-emitter is permitted to construct a REQ. |
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:205:| `defer_outbound` (requests.rs:558-568) | Moves to the wire-emitter; deferral is per-relay, keyed by URL, not by role. |

 1. Read this PLAN + your section's brief + cited prereqs (briefs or drafted sections) before drafting.

 5. Include ≥3 anti-patterns and ≥2 concrete deliverables from the brief.
 6. End every section with `See also:` listing `xref` exactly as `[NN — title](NN-name.md)`.

 ## 3.1 Stage 1 — Resolve authors to mailboxes
 
-Inputs: every `LogicalInterest` with non-empty `shape.authors` or non-empty `shape.tags[#p]`; the mailbox cache populated by `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233`).
+Inputs: every `LogicalInterest` with non-empty `shape.authors` or non-empty `shape.tags[#p]`; the mailbox cache populated by `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest/relay_list.rs:25-63`).
 
 Output: an `AuthorRouting` per author per direction:
 
@@ -110,6 +110,7 @@
 6. `A.lifecycle == B.lifecycle`. Tailing and one-shot do not merge (one-shot would never close).
 7. `A.event_ids` and `B.event_ids`: merge by union, capped at the relay's per-filter `ids` limit.
 8. **Rule 8 (address-pointer union).** `A.addresses` and `B.addresses` merge by `A.addresses ∪ B.addresses`, provided their other constraints (`authors`, `kinds`, `tags`, time, lifecycle) merge per Rules 1–7. Overlapping coordinates with differing time or lifecycle constraints do **not** merge — the compiler emits per-address sub-shapes so each `NaddrCoord`'s routing is independent. The union cap is the relay's per-filter `#a` value limit (default 1000). Address routing uses `NaddrCoord::pubkey` as the `AuthorRouting` input (Outbox direction, Stage 1), so the relays chosen are the addressed author's write relays — the same path as an `authors`-bearing filter for that pubkey.
+9. **Rule 9 (`relay_pin` equality).** `A.relay_pin == B.relay_pin`; `None` does not absorb `Some(_)`. Host-pinned interests route through Case E before Cases A-D, and identical pins may still coalesce via Rules 1-8.
 
 When mergeable, the merged shape is `{ authors: A.authors ∪ B.authors, addresses: A.addresses ∪ B.addresses, ... }`. The merged interest tracks both originating `InterestId`s so per-event dispatch back to consumers stays correct.
 
@@ -168,7 +169,7 @@
 
 Properties:
 
-- **Recompilation with no change ⇒ same plan-id.** If `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:218-221`) deduplicates and decides not to replace a stale mailbox, no plan-id churn.
+- **Recompilation with no change ⇒ same plan-id.** If `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest/relay_list.rs:38-61`) deduplicates and decides not to replace a stale mailbox, no plan-id churn.
 - **Adding an interest changes plan-id even if no new wire REQ results.** Two interests can merge into the same SubShape; the plan-id changes because the *interest set* changed. The platform diagnostic correctly reports "logical-interest count went up; wire-sub count did not."
 - **A new kind:10002 for a referenced author changes plan-id.** The hash only covers mailboxes for authors the current interest set touches, so a kind:10002 for an unrelated pubkey (not in any active interest's `authors` or `#p`) does not churn plan-ids.
 - **A new kind:10002 for an unreferenced author does NOT change plan-id.** This is the key
@@ -183,7 +184,7 @@

 1. Read this PLAN + your section's brief + cited prereqs (briefs or drafted sections) before drafting.
 2. Honor the `status` field strictly. SHIPS may cite `crates/`/`apps/`/`ios/` `path:line`; LANDED cites design + ADRs + partial code; PLANNED cites plan files + ADRs only.
-3. Verify every `path:line` you cite by Reading the file in the same commit (`a92ed2e` or later on master).
+3. Verify every `path:line` you cite by reading the file at the current master tip.
 4. Keep your file ≤ 300 LOC. If exceeded, split per the brief's hint (e.g. 05a/05b) and update the TOC in a follow-up.
 5. Include ≥3 anti-patterns and ≥2 concrete deliverables from the brief.
 6. End every section with `See also:` listing `xref` exactly as `[NN — title](NN-name.md)`.

 ## 3.1 Stage 1 — Resolve authors to mailboxes
 
-Inputs: every `LogicalInterest` with non-empty `shape.authors` or non-empty `shape.tags[#p]`; the mailbox cache populated by `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233`).
+Inputs: every `LogicalInterest` with non-empty `shape.authors` or non-empty `shape.tags[#p]`; the mailbox cache populated by `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest/relay_list.rs:25-63`).
 
 Output: an `AuthorRouting` per author per direction:
 
@@ -110,6 +110,7 @@
 6. `A.lifecycle == B.lifecycle`. Tailing and one-shot do not merge (one-shot would never close).
 7. `A.event_ids` and `B.event_ids`: merge by union, capped at the relay's per-filter `ids` limit.
 8. **Rule 8 (address-pointer union).** `A.addresses` and `B.addresses` merge by `A.addresses ∪ B.addresses`, provided their other constraints (`authors`, `kinds`, `tags`, time, lifecycle) merge per Rules 1–7. Overlapping coordinates with differing time or lifecycle constraints do **not** merge — the compiler emits per-address sub-shapes so each `NaddrCoord`'s routing is independent. The union cap is the relay's per-filter `#a` value limit (default 1000). Address routing uses `NaddrCoord::pubkey` as the `AuthorRouting` input (Outbox direction, Stage 1), so the relays chosen are the addressed author's write relays — the same path as an `authors`-bearing filter for that pubkey.
+9. **Rule 9 (`relay_pin` equality).** `A.relay_pin == B.relay_pin`; `None` does not absorb `Some(_)`. Host-pinned interests route through Case E before Cases A-D, and identical pins may still coalesce via Rules 1-8.
 
 When mergeable, the merged shape is `{ authors: A.authors ∪ B.authors, addresses: A.addresses ∪ B.addresses, ... }`. The merged interest tracks both originating `InterestId`s so per-event dispatch back to consumers stays correct.
 
@@ -168,7 +169,7 @@
 
 Properties:
 
-- **Recompilation with no change ⇒ same plan-id.** If `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:218-221`) deduplicates and decides not to replace a stale mailbox, no plan-id churn.
+- **Recompilation with no change ⇒ same plan-id.** If `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest/relay_list.rs:38-61`) deduplicates and decides not to replace a stale mailbox, no plan-id churn.
 - **Adding an interest changes plan-id even if no new wire REQ results.** Two interests can merge into the same SubShape; the plan-id changes because the *interest set* changed. The platform diagnostic correctly reports "logical-interest count went up; wire-sub count did not."
 - **A new kind:10002 for a referenced author changes plan-id.** The hash only covers mailboxes for authors the current interest set touches, so a kind:10002 for an unrelated pubkey (not in any active interest's `authors` or `#p`) does not churn plan-ids.
 - **A new kind:10002 for an unreferenced author does NOT change plan-id.** This is the key

 1. Read this PLAN + your section's brief + cited prereqs (briefs or drafted sections) before drafting.
 2. Honor the `status` field strictly. SHIPS may cite `crates/`/`apps/`/`ios/` `path:line`; LANDED cites design + ADRs + partial code; PLANNED cites plan files + ADRs only.

 4. Keep your file ≤ 300 LOC. If exceeded, split per the brief's hint (e.g. 05a/05b) and update the TOC in a follow-up.
 5. Include ≥3 anti-patterns and ≥2 concrete deliverables from the brief.
 6. End every section with `See also:` listing `xref` exactly as `[NN — title](NN-name.md)`.

-The wire-emitter (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/wire.rs`, to be added) diffs the new plan against the current wire-sub registry: opens new REQs, closes orphaned ones, leaves stable assignments untouched.
+The wire-emitter (`crates/nmp-core/src/subs/wire.rs`) diffs the new plan against the current wire-sub registry: opens new REQs, closes orphaned ones, leaves stable assignments untouched.
 
 ## 3.1 Stage 1 — Resolve authors to mailboxes
 
-Inputs: every `LogicalInterest` with non-empty `shape.authors` or non-empty `shape.tags[#p]`; the mailbox cache populated by `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233`).
+Inputs: every `LogicalInterest` with non-empty `shape.authors` or non-empty `shape.tags[#p]`; the mailbox cache populated by `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest/relay_list.rs:25-63`).
 
 Output: an `AuthorRouting` per author per direction:
 
@@ -110,6 +110,7 @@
 6. `A.lifecycle == B.lifecycle`. Tailing and one-shot do not merge (one-shot would never close).
 7. `A.event_ids` and `B.event_ids`: merge by union, capped at the relay's per-filter `ids` limit.
 8. **Rule 8 (address-pointer union).** `A.addresses` and `B.addresses` merge by `A.addresses ∪ B.addresses`, provided their other constraints (`authors`, `kinds`, `tags`, time, lifecycle) merge per Rules 1–7. Overlapping coordinates with differing time or lifecycle constraints do **not** merge — the compiler emits per-address sub-shapes so each `NaddrCoord`'s routing is independent. The union cap is the relay's per-filter `#a` value limit (default 1000). Address routing uses `NaddrCoord::pubkey` as the `AuthorRouting` input (Outbox direction, Stage 1), so the relays chosen are the addressed author's write relays — the same path as an `authors`-bearing filter for that pubkey.
+9. **Rule 9 (`relay_pin` equality).** `A.relay_pin == B.relay_pin`; `None` does not absorb `Some(_)`. Host-pinned interests route through Case E before Cases A-D, and identical pins may still coalesce via Rules 1-8.
 
 When mergeable, the merged shape is `{ authors: A.authors ∪ B.authors, addresses: A.addresses ∪ B.addresses, ... }`. The merged interest tracks both originating `InterestId`s so per-event dispatch back to consumers stays correct.
 
@@ -168,7 +169,7 @@
 
 Properties:
 
-- **Recompilation with no change ⇒ same plan-id.** If `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:218-221`) deduplicates and decides not to replace a stale mailbox, no plan-id churn.
+- **Recompilation with no change ⇒ same plan-id.** If `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest/relay_list.rs:38-61`) deduplicates and decides not to replace a stale mailbox, no plan-id churn.
 - **Adding an interest changes plan-id even if no new wire REQ results.** Two interests can merge into the same SubShape; the plan-id changes because the *interest set* changed. The platform diagnostic correctly reports "logical-interest count went up; wire-sub count did not."
 - **A new kind:10002 for a referenced author changes plan-id.** The hash only covers mailboxes for authors the current interest set touches, so a kind:10002 for an unrelated pubkey (not in any active interest's `authors` or `#p`) does not churn plan-ids.
 - **A new kind:10002 for an unreferenced author does NOT change plan-id.** This is the key
@@ -183,7 +184,7 @@
 
 ## 3.5 Migration of existing functions
 
-This is the binding contract: each function in `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs` and `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs` either disappears, becomes thin glue over the compiler, or graduates into a typed module. The compiler does not coexist with the old planner; M2 replaces it.
+This is the binding contract: each function in `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests/` and `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest/` either disappears, becomes thin glue over the compiler, or graduates into a typed module. The compiler does not coexist with the old planner; M2 replaces it.
 
 | Current function (file:line) | M2 replacement |
 |---|---|

 1. Read this PLAN + your section's brief + cited prereqs (briefs or drafted sections) before drafting.
 2. Honor the `status` field strictly. SHIPS may cite `crates/`/`apps/`/`ios/` `path:line`; LANDED cites design + ADRs + partial code; PLANNED cites plan files + ADRs only.

 5. Include ≥3 anti-patterns and ≥2 concrete deliverables from the brief.
 6. End every section with `See also:` listing `xref` exactly as `[NN — title](NN-name.md)`.

 ## 3.1 Stage 1 — Resolve authors to mailboxes
 
-Inputs: every `LogicalInterest` with non-empty `shape.authors` or non-empty `shape.tags[#p]`; the mailbox cache populated by `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233`).
+Inputs: every `LogicalInterest` with non-empty `shape.authors` or non-empty `shape.tags[#p]`; the mailbox cache populated by `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest/relay_list.rs:25-63`).
 
 Output: an `AuthorRouting` per author per direction:
 
@@ -110,6 +110,7 @@
 6. `A.lifecycle == B.lifecycle`. Tailing and one-shot do not merge (one-shot would never close).
 7. `A.event_ids` and `B.event_ids`: merge by union, capped at the relay's per-filter `ids` limit.
 8. **Rule 8 (address-pointer union).** `A.addresses` and `B.addresses` merge by `A.addresses ∪ B.addresses`, provided their other constraints (`authors`, `kinds`, `tags`, time, lifecycle) merge per Rules 1–7. Overlapping coordinates with differing time or lifecycle constraints do **not** merge — the compiler emits per-address sub-shapes so each `NaddrCoord`'s routing is independent. The union cap is the relay's per-filter `#a` value limit (default 1000). Address routing uses `NaddrCoord::pubkey` as the `AuthorRouting` input (Outbox direction, Stage 1), so the relays chosen are the addressed author's write relays — the same path as an `authors`-bearing filter for that pubkey.
+9. **Rule 9 (`relay_pin` equality).** `A.relay_pin == B.relay_pin`; `None` does not absorb `Some(_)`. Host-pinned interests route through Case E before Cases A-D, and identical pins may still coalesce via Rules 1-8.
 
 When mergeable, the merged shape is `{ authors: A.authors ∪ B.authors, addresses: A.addresses ∪ B.addresses, ... }`. The merged interest tracks both originating `InterestId`s so per-event dispatch back to consumers stays correct.
 
@@ -168,7 +169,7 @@
 
 Properties:
 
-- **Recompilation with no change ⇒ same plan-id.** If `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:218-221`) deduplicates and decides not to replace a stale mailbox, no plan-id churn.
+- **Recompilation with no change ⇒ same plan-id.** If `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest/relay_list.rs:38-61`) deduplicates and decides not to replace a stale mailbox, no plan-id churn.
 - **Adding an interest changes plan-id even if no new wire REQ results.** Two interests can merge into the same SubShape; the plan-id changes because the *interest set* changed. The platform diagnostic correctly reports "logical-interest count went up; wire-sub count did not."
 - **A new kind:10002 for a referenced author changes plan-id.** The hash only covers mailboxes for authors the current interest set touches, so a kind:10002 for an unrelated pubkey (not in any active interest's `authors` or `#p`) does not churn plan-ids.
 - **A new kind:10002 for an unreferenced author does NOT change plan-id.** This is the key
@@ -183,7 +184,7 @@
 
 ## 3.5 Migration of existing functions
 
-This is the binding contract: each function in `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs` and `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs` either disappears, becomes thin glue over the compiler, or graduates into a typed module. The compiler does not coexist with the old planner; M2 replaces it.
+This is the binding contract: each function in `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests/` and `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest/` either disappears, becomes thin glue over the compiler, or graduates into a typed module. The compiler does not coexist with the old planner; M2 replaces it.
 
 | Current function (file:line) | M2 replacement |
 |---|---|

 ## 3.1 Stage 1 — Resolve authors to mailboxes
 
-Inputs: every `LogicalInterest` with non-empty `shape.authors` or non-empty `shape.tags[#p]`; the mailbox cache populated by `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233`).
+Inputs: every `LogicalInterest` with non-empty `shape.authors` or non-empty `shape.tags[#p]`; the mailbox cache populated by `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest/relay_list.rs:25-63`).
 
 Output: an `AuthorRouting` per author per direction:
 
@@ -110,6 +110,7 @@
 6. `A.lifecycle == B.lifecycle`. Tailing and one-shot do not merge (one-shot would never close).
 7. `A.event_ids` and `B.event_ids`: merge by union, capped at the relay's per-filter `ids` limit.
 8. **Rule 8 (address-pointer union).** `A.addresses` and `B.addresses` merge by `A.addresses ∪ B.addresses`, provided their other constraints (`authors`, `kinds`, `tags`, time, lifecycle) merge per Rules 1–7. Overlapping coordinates with differing time or lifecycle constraints do **not** merge — the compiler emits per-address sub-shapes so each `NaddrCoord`'s routing is independent. The union cap is the relay's per-filter `#a` value limit (default 1000). Address routing uses `NaddrCoord::pubkey` as the `AuthorRouting` input (Outbox direction, Stage 1), so the relays chosen are the addressed author's write relays — the same path as an `authors`-bearing filter for that pubkey.
+9. **Rule 9 (`relay_pin` equality).** `A.relay_pin == B.relay_pin`; `None` does not absorb `Some(_)`. Host-pinned interests route through Case E before Cases A-D, and identical pins may still coalesce via Rules 1-8.
 
 When mergeable, the merged shape is `{ authors: A.authors ∪ B.authors, addresses: A.addresses ∪ B.addresses, ... }`. The merged interest tracks both originating `InterestId`s so per-event dispatch back to consumers stays correct.
 
@@ -168,7 +169,7 @@
 
 Properties:
 
-- **Recompilation with no change ⇒ same plan-id.** If `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:218-221`) deduplicates and decides not to replace a stale mailbox, no plan-id churn.
+- **Recompilation with no change ⇒ same plan-id.** If `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest/relay_list.rs:38-61`) deduplicates and decides not to replace a stale mailbox, no plan-id churn.
 - **Adding an interest changes plan-id even if no new wire REQ results.** Two interests can merge into the same SubShape; the plan-id changes because the *interest set* changed. The platform diagnostic correctly reports "logical-interest count went up; wire-sub count did not."
 - **A new kind:10002 for a referenced author changes plan-id.** The hash only covers mailboxes for authors the current interest set touches, so a kind:10002 for an unrelated pubkey (not in any active interest's `authors` or `#p`) does not churn plan-ids.
 - **A new kind:10002 for an unreferenced author does NOT change plan-id.** This is the key
@@ -183,7 +184,7 @@
 
 ## 3.5 Migration of existing functions
 
-This is the binding contract: each function in `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs` and `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs` either disappears, becomes thin glue over the compiler, or graduates into a typed module. The compiler does not coexist with the old planner; M2 replaces it.
+This is the binding contract: each function in `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests/` and `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest/` either disappears, becomes thin glue over the compiler, or graduates into a typed module. The compiler does not coexist with the old planner; M2 replaces it.

 |---|---|

 1. Read this PLAN + your section's brief + cited prereqs (briefs or drafted sections) before drafting.
 2. Honor the `status` field strictly. SHIPS may cite `crates/`/`apps/`/`ios/` `path:line`; LANDED cites design + ADRs + partial code; PLANNED cites plan files + ADRs only.
-3. Verify every `path:line` you cite by Reading the file in the same commit (`a92ed2e` or later on master).
+3. Verify every `path:line` you cite by reading the file at the current master tip.
 4. Keep your file ≤ 300 LOC. If exceeded, split per the brief's hint (e.g. 05a/05b) and update the TOC in a follow-up.
 5. Include ≥3 anti-patterns and ≥2 concrete deliverables from the brief.
 6. End every section with `See also:` listing `xref` exactly as `[NN — title](NN-name.md)`.

 ## 3.1 Stage 1 — Resolve authors to mailboxes
 
-Inputs: every `LogicalInterest` with non-empty `shape.authors` or non-empty `shape.tags[#p]`; the mailbox cache populated by `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233`).
+Inputs: every `LogicalInterest` with non-empty `shape.authors` or non-empty `shape.tags[#p]`; the mailbox cache populated by `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest/relay_list.rs:25-63`).
 
 Output: an `AuthorRouting` per author per direction:
 
@@ -110,6 +110,7 @@
 6. `A.lifecycle == B.lifecycle`. Tailing and one-shot do not merge (one-shot would never close).
 7. `A.event_ids` and `B.event_ids`: merge by union, capped at the relay's per-filter `ids` limit.
 8. **Rule 8 (address-pointer union).** `A.addresses` and `B.addresses` merge by `A.addresses ∪ B.addresses`, provided their other constraints (`authors`, `kinds`, `tags`, time, lifecycle) merge per Rules 1–7. Overlapping coordinates with differing time or lifecycle constraints do **not** merge — the compiler emits per-address sub-shapes so each `NaddrCoord`'s routing is independent. The union cap is the relay's per-filter `#a` value limit (default 1000). Address routing uses `NaddrCoord::pubkey` as the `AuthorRouting` input (Outbox direction, Stage 1), so the relays chosen are the addressed author's write relays — the same path as an `authors`-bearing filter for that pubkey.
+9. **Rule 9 (`relay_pin` equality).** `A.relay_pin == B.relay_pin`; `None` does not absorb `Some(_)`. Host-pinned interests route through Case E before Cases A-D, and identical pins may still coalesce via Rules 1-8.
 
 When mergeable, the merged shape is `{ authors: A.authors ∪ B.authors, addresses: A.addresses ∪ B.addresses, ... }`. The merged interest tracks both originating `InterestId`s so per-event dispatch back to consumers stays correct.
 
@@ -168,7 +169,7 @@
 
 Properties:
 
-- **Recompilation with no change ⇒ same plan-id.** If `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:218-221`) deduplicates and decides not to replace a stale mailbox, no plan-id churn.
+- **Recompilation with no change ⇒ same plan-id.** If `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest/relay_list.rs:38-61`) deduplicates and decides not to replace a stale mailbox, no plan-id churn.
 - **Adding an interest changes plan-id even if no new wire REQ results.** Two interests can merge into the same SubShape; the plan-id changes because the *interest set* changed. The platform diagnostic correctly reports "logical-interest count went up; wire-sub count did not."
 - **A new kind:10002 for a referenced author changes plan-id.** The hash only covers mailboxes for authors the current interest set touches, so a kind:10002 for an unrelated pubkey (not in any active interest's `authors` or `#p`) does not churn plan-ids.
 - **A new kind:10002 for an unreferenced author does NOT change plan-id.** This is the key
@@ -183,7 +184,7 @@
 
 ## 3.5 Migration of existing functions
 
-This is the binding contract: each function in `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs` and `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs` either disappears, becomes thin glue over the compiler, or graduates into a typed module. The compiler does not coexist with the old planner; M2 replaces it.
+This is the binding contract: each function in `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests/` and `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest/` either disappears, becomes thin glue over the compiler, or graduates into a typed module. The compiler does not coexist with the old planner; M2 replaces it.

 ## 3.1 Stage 1 — Resolve authors to mailboxes
 
-Inputs: every `LogicalInterest` with non-empty `shape.authors` or non-empty `shape.tags[#p]`; the mailbox cache populated by `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233`).
+Inputs: every `LogicalInterest` with non-empty `shape.authors` or non-empty `shape.tags[#p]`; the mailbox cache populated by `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest/relay_list.rs:25-63`).
 
 Output: an `AuthorRouting` per author per direction:
 
@@ -110,6 +110,7 @@
 6. `A.lifecycle == B.lifecycle`. Tailing and one-shot do not merge (one-shot would never close).
 7. `A.event_ids` and `B.event_ids`: merge by union, capped at the relay's per-filter `ids` limit.
 8. **Rule 8 (address-pointer union).** `A.addresses` and `B.addresses` merge by `A.addresses ∪ B.addresses`, provided their other constraints (`authors`, `kinds`, `tags`, time, lifecycle) merge per Rules 1–7. Overlapping coordinates with differing time or lifecycle constraints do **not** merge — the compiler emits per-address sub-shapes so each `NaddrCoord`'s routing is independent. The union cap is the relay's per-filter `#a` value limit (default 1000). Address routing uses `NaddrCoord::pubkey` as the `AuthorRouting` input (Outbox direction, Stage 1), so the relays chosen are the addressed author's write relays — the same path as an `authors`-bearing filter for that pubkey.
+9. **Rule 9 (`relay_pin` equality).** `A.relay_pin == B.relay_pin`; `None` does not absorb `Some(_)`. Host-pinned interests route through Case E before Cases A-D, and identical pins may still coalesce via Rules 1-8.
 
 When mergeable, the merged shape is `{ authors: A.authors ∪ B.authors, addresses: A.addresses ∪ B.addresses, ... }`. The merged interest tracks both originating `InterestId`s so per-event dispatch back to consumers stays correct.
 
@@ -168,7 +169,7 @@
 
 Properties:
 
-- **Recompilation with no change ⇒ same plan-id.** If `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:218-221`) deduplicates and decides not to replace a stale mailbox, no plan-id churn.
+- **Recompilation with no change ⇒ same plan-id.** If `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest/relay_list.rs:38-61`) deduplicates and decides not to replace a stale mailbox, no plan-id churn.
 - **Adding an interest changes plan-id even if no new wire REQ results.** Two interests can merge into the same SubShape; the plan-id changes because the *interest set* changed. The platform diagnostic correctly reports "logical-interest count went up; wire-sub count did not."
 - **A new kind:10002 for a referenced author changes plan-id.** The hash only covers mailboxes for authors the current interest set touches, so a kind:10002 for an unrelated pubkey (not in any active interest's `authors` or `#p`) does not churn plan-ids.
 - **A new kind:10002 for an unreferenced author does NOT change plan-id.** This is the key
@@ -183,7 +184,7 @@
 
 ## 3.5 Migration of existing functions
 
-This is the binding contract: each function in `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs` and `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs` either disappears, becomes thin glue over the compiler, or graduates into a typed module. The compiler does not coexist with the old planner; M2 replaces it.
+This is the binding contract: each function in `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests/` and `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest/` either disappears, becomes thin glue over the compiler, or graduates into a typed module. The compiler does not coexist with the old planner; M2 replaces it.
